volume.h1:
SOUTH INDIAN INSCRIPTIONSlbVolume XXII
Part II

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(A.R. No. 303 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—31st YEAR (A.D. 937-38)
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of land for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Perumānaḍigaḷ at Gōvindapāḍi in Valla-nāḍu by Amudan Peṟṟāḷ alias Pallavan-
Mādēviyār of Kāṇattūr in Umbaḷa-nāḍu the queen of Śōḻapperumānaḍigaḷ
(Parāntaka).

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] matirai koṇṭa kopparakēsaripanmaṟku

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(A.R. No. 304 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
PĀRTHIVĒNDRAVARMAN—3rd YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is a damaged epigraph recording a gift of gold for a perpetual lamp
in the temple of Gōvindapāḍi-Āḷvār by one Nārāyaṇa-Kramavittan alias Vaidumba-
rāditya-Brahmādhirājaṉ of Ikkāṭṭukkōṭṭam, who deposited the amount with the
sabhā of Vallam in Dāmarkōṭṭam for the necessary supply of oil for the purpose,
by the liquid measure Ūragattuniṉṟār-kāl.

valladvijasabhamayigōvi••••bhyōka•••

yāṅkaḷ koṇṭu kaṭava poṉ ••••••

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(A.R. No. 305 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—10th YEAR (A.D 994-95)
inscription.p:
This is a damaged fragment of a record stopping with the mention of the
dēvakanmi (of the temple) and of Dāmalkōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(A.R. No. 306 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN—3rd YEAR
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XIII, No. 33.

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(A.R. No. 307 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
MADIRAIKOṆḌA RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN (SUNDARA-CHŌḶA PARĀNTAKA II)
—17th YEAR (A.D. 968-69)
inscription.p:
This records a joint gift by three persons providing for burning a per-
petual lamp in the temple of Gōvindapāḍi-Perumānaḍigaḷ.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] matiraikoṇṭa kovirājake-

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(A.R. No. 308 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
MADIRAIKOṆḌA RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN (SUNDARA-CHŌḶA PARĀNTAKA II)
—17th YEAR (A.D. 968-69)
inscription.p:
This also records a gift for a perpetual lamp of 96 sheep by one Kēsavan
Rāmaṉ alias Śembiyaṉ Panaiyūrnāṭṭu-Vēḷāṉ of Panaiyūr in Panaiyūr-nāḍu a division
of Śōḻanāḍu on the southern bank of the river (kāvērī), to the temple at
Gōvindapāḍi.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] matiraikoṇṭa kovirāja-

inscription.head:
No. 309.
(A.R. No. 309 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—31st YEAR (A.D. 937-38)
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It seems to record a gift made to the temple at
Gōvindapāḍi, of a gold salver to hold the sacred offerings to the deity, by the
king on the day of a solar eclipse, when he visited the temple and made a
present of svanamēdani (suvarṇamēdinī) (?) to the god.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] matiraikoṇṭa koppara kēsaripanmarkku
yāṇṭu 31- vatu sūryakkirāṇa-

inscription.head:
No. 310.
(A.R. No. 310 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—40th YEAR (A.D. 966-67)
inscription.p:
The king is called Parakēsarivarman “who took Madirai and Īḻam”. This
is also damaged and perhaps also engraved by an ignorant scribe. It registers
an endowment of 2000 kuḻi of land after purchase by Dēvaṉ Gōvindaṉ alias
Pirānta[ka*] Anukki Śōḻanāyakaṉ of Pūḷikuḍi (?) in Śēṟṟūr-kūṟṟam for burning
a perpetual lamp in the temple at Gōvindapāḍi in Valla-nāḍu.

edition.l: svasti śrī [*] matiraiyum īḻamuṅ koṇṭa [kopparakecaripaṉma]ṟku

inscription.head:
No. 311.
(A.R. No. 311 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—34th YEAR (A.D. 940-41)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 30 poṉ for making a crown to (the image god)
Gōvindapāḍi-Perumānaḍigaḷ by Oḻukkai Vīranārāyaṇaṉ alias Anantavikrama-
Pallavaraiyar probably an officer of the king.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] [matiraiko]-

inscription.head:
No. 312.
(A.R. No. 312 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN—3rd YEAR
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XIII, No. 34.

inscription.head:
No. 313.
(A.R. No. 313 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I (PARAKĒSARIVARMAN who took Madirai and Īḻam)
—41st YEAR (A.D. 947-48)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 100 sheep for a perpetual lamp in the temple of
Perumānaḍigaḷ at Gōvindapāḍi by Neḍiyataḷi Pūvaṉ Māṟaṉ a native of Koḍuṅgōḷūr
in Malaināḍu.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] matiraiyu miḻamuṅ koṇṭa kopparakecaripaṉmaṟkku yā-

l.ta: ṇṭu nāṟpattoṉṟāvatu śrī kovintapāṭi niṉṟaruḷiṉa perumā-
yiṣṇavan tiruvaṭiye paṉṉeṭuṅkālamuṅ kāttār śrī vaiku

inscription.head:
No. 314.
(A.R. No. 314 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—31st YEAR (A.D. 937-38)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of a perpetual lamp to the Perumānaḍigaḷ at Gōvindapāḍi
in Valla-nāḍu a division of Dāmarkōṭṭam in Toṇḍai-nāḍu, by queen Amanimā-
dēviyār on behalf of her elder sister Kambaṉ Guṇakānti. The donor is also
called Rāśingam and referred to as a Manavāsi (native) of Poḻiyūr in Poḻiyūr-
nāḍu a division of Pāṇḍi-nāḍu.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] matir[ai koṇ]ṭa kopparakecari pa-

l.ta: kaiyār kampaṅ kuṇakāntiyaic cāttit toṇṭaiya nāṭ-

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(A.R. No. 315 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA—Date lost.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of half a māḍai of gold for burning a lamp during the
service in the temple of Śrīkōvilanār at Gōvindapāḍi in Valla-nāḍu a division of
Dāmarkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam by Vīraśōḻaṉ Tiruvaraṅgamuḍaiyāṉ a
resident of a tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam village (name lost) belonging to the temple. The
amount was entrusted with a priest of the temple by name Uyyavandāṉ Bhaṭṭan
of Kāñjiram.

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(A.R. No. 316 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA—10th YEAR
inscription.p:
This is damaged. It seems to record some gift made by Vēṭṭandai Seṭṭi
alias Gaṇavadiyāṇḍāṉ a merchant of Pūndamalli in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam for the early
morning service of the deity and for burning two lamps at the time in the temple
at Gōvindavāḍi.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravatti-

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(A.R. No. 317 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶA I—21st YEAR (A.D. 1032-33)
inscription.p:
This begins with introduction Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara etc. It is left incomplete
with the mention of some provision by way of land gift for the expenses of a
festival (?) on the day of dvādaśī in the Mārgaḻi month every year in the temple
of Perumānaḍigaḷ “who was pleased to reside at Gōvindapāḍi”.

svasti śrī [||*] tirumaṉṉi vaḷara irunilamaṭantaiyum porcceyappāvaiyum

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(A.R. No. 318 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—Date lost.
inscription.p:
This is a mere fragment of an inscription. Below it are found two lines
of writing which mention some agreement (ōṭṭōlai) given by Oṟṟi Rājādittaṉ a
resident of the village and by some others.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] matiraikoṇṭa kopparakecaripaṉmaṟku yāṇ*
*ṣtops here• Below this are the following two lines.

l.ta:
taṉ ivai yen ṉeḻuttu ||—ippaṭi yoṭṭi oṭṭolai iṭṭukuṭutten
ivvūr koṭṭayakiḻān oṟṟi rājā-

tittaneṉ ivai yeṉ neḻuttu:—ippaṭiyo[ṭ*]ṭi oṭṭolai iṭṭu
kuṭuttom tiyan māppeṟṟaṭika—

inscription.head:
No. 319.
(A.R. No. 319 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARĀNTAKA I—12th YEAR (A.D. 918-19)
inscription.p:
This registers an endowment (details not given) made by one Māyāṉ
Perumāṉ of Pūñjiṟṟūr in Kiḻār-kūṟṟam for the daily feeding of five Brāhmaṇas
in the maṭha (attached to the temple) of Perumānaḍigaḷ at Govindapāḍi in
Toṇḍai-nāḍu.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] matiraikoṇṭa kopparakecari(ppari) panmaṟku yāṇṭu
paṉṉiraṇṭāvatu kiḻāṟkūṟṟattup pūñciṟṟūr-

l.ta:
ruṭaiyāṉ māyāṉ perumāṉ toṇṭaināṭṭu cirikovintapāṭip
perumāṉaṭi-

maṭam irakṣippāṉukkeyvatāka vaitteṉ māyāṉ perumāṉeṉ

inscription.head:
No. 320-321.
(A.R. Nos. 320 & 321 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN—13th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XIX, Nos. 318 & 319.

inscription.head:
No. 322.
(A.R. No. 322 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—19th YEAR (A.D. 1003-04)
inscription.p:
The inscription has the introduction Tirumagaḷ pōla etc. It states that one
thousand kuḻi of land at Śiṟṟiyāṟṟūr belonging to the temple of Gōvindapāḍi-
Perumānaḍigaḷ, which had been overlaid with sand on account of a breach in
the Pālār river was brought under cultivation by the voluntary efforts of
Kaḍambaṉ Veṇkāḍaṉ a Veḷḷāḷa a resident of Nallūr-Pudukkuḍi in Tiruvaḻundūr-
nāḍu a division of Śōḻanāḍu. He was helped in this by an additional allotment
of temple lands adjoining the above with irrigational facilities. Kaḍambaṉ
Veṇkāḍaṉ then re-endowed the whole plot of land for providing for the daily
sacred offerings to the deity with rice, ghee and other specified articles. The
inscription also records another gift 1150 kuḻi of land by the same donor after
purchasing the same from the Ūrār of Veṇpākkam for burning a perpetuaḷ lamp
in the temple.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tirumakaḷpolap perunilaccelviyum taṉakke yurimai
pūṇṭamaiy ma-

l.ta:
yum taṭikaipāṭiyum kuṭamalaināṭuṅ kollamum kaliṅkamum eṇṭikai
pukaḻtara īḻa-

inscription.head:
No. 323-324.
(A.R. No. 323 & 324 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
PĀRTHIVĒNDRAVARMAN—12th YEAR.
inscription.p:
Both the impressions and the transcripts of these two inscriptions are
now missing. In the Annual Report for 1907, List of inscriptions, the first
epigraph is stated to be in prose and verse and to record that Anaiyamaṉ
Paramaṇḍalādittaṉ bulit a maṇḍapa in the temple. The second is said to be
damaged and to record a gift by the Virāṭa king Anaiyamaṉ. His ancestors are
said to be mentioned as in No. 267 above.

inscription.head:
No. 325.
(A.R. No. 325 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN—Date lost.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XIII, No. 330.

inscription.head:
No. 326.
(A.R. No. 326 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This consists of a number of disconnected pieces of one or more inscrip-
tions. The first piece mentions a sale of land tax-free, by the sabhā of Arumoḻi-
dēva-chaturvēdimaṅgalam to the temple of Perumānaḍigaḷ at Gōvindapāḍi.
Piece 2 refers to some persons reciting the puṇyapam (?). Piece 5 mentions
some items of requirements of worship to the deity on the day of Tiruvādirai
in Mārgaḻi month. Piece 6 similarly lists some requirements of worship and
refers to four persons reciting the Tiruvāymoḻi in the temple and to 50 persons
versed in the vedic lore. It quotes the 21st year of a king whose name is lost
and ends with the mention of a sale of land tax-free by the assembly of
Veṇpākkam to the temple at Govindapāḍi for the gold received by them.

inscription.head:
No. 327.
(A.R. No. 327 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
PĀRTHIVĒNDRAVARMAN who took the head of Vīra-Pāṇḍya—10th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in the
temple by one Vāṇarājaṉ Aḷakamaiyaṉ.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] virapāṇṭiyaṉait talaiykoṇṭa koppāttiventi-
rātiṭanma[ṟ*]kku yāṇṭu 10- ttāvatu peruma•••• ṟayaṉākaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 328.
(A.R. No. 328 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PĀRTHIVĒNDRĀDIVARMAN—13th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of 2000 kuḻi of land at Śiṟṟiyāṟṟūr in Mēl-
Paḷugūr-nāḍu a division of Maṇaiyil-kōṭṭam, left in charge of the Vaishṇavadāsar
managing the temple of Gōvindapāḍi-Perumānaḍigaḷ by Mannaṉ Kaṇṇaṉ alias
Kāmarmōha-Vāraṇappēraraiyar the elephant mahout of the king, for the purpose
of feeding one Brāhmaṇa daily in the maṭha (attached to the temple). The
land is said to have been purchased by the donor from the temple itself.

inscription.head:
No. 329.
(A.R. No. 329 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJAKĒSARIVARMAN—5th YEAR
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XIII, No. 105.

inscription.head:
No. 330.
(A.R. No. 330 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJĀDHIRĀJADĒVA (I)—32nd YEAR (A.D. 1049-50)
inscription.p:
The inscription is much damaged. It begins with the introduction
Tiṅgaḷēr taru etc. It registers a sale of land free of taxes, by the Ūrār of
Amaṇpākkam in Paḷugūr-nāḍu a division of Maṇaiyiṟkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa
maṇḍalam in favour of god Uyyakkoṇḍāḻvār at Gōvindapāḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 331.
(A.R. No. 331 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
The writing of the inscription seems to be of about the 11th century A.D.
This is built in at the beginning. It seems to record an agreement given by
a servant of the temple of Gōvindapāḍi-Perumānaḍigaḷ undertaking to supply
4 nāḻi of rice daily to the temple (for offerings) as interest on 45 kaḻañju of
gold received by him from Chandrabhūti-Chaturvēdi-Sōmayāji, a resident of
Kōṭṭūr in Mērpaṅgā-nāḍu a division of Maṇaiyiṟkōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(A.R. No. 332 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA—13th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is fragmentary. It mentions a certain Tiruvēṅgaḍadēvaṉ, a native
of Vikramaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Puḻaṟkōṭṭam, a sub-division of Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] tirupuva-
13- vatu je[ya*]ṅkoṇ•••••

inscription.head:
No. 333.
(A.R. No. 333 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—10th YEAR (A.D. 994-95)
inscription.p:
This records an endowment of 20 kaḻañju of gold by Arunilai Śrīkṛishṇaṉ
alias Mūvēnda Piḍavūrvēḷār the accountant of Piḍavūr-nāḍu in Śōḻanāḍu, which
was invested with the Ūrār of Vēḷkaḷa Veṇpākkam adjacent to (?) Gōvindapāḍi.
The three kaḻañju collected from them as interest every year was to be utilised
by the trustees of the temple, for the recitation of the Tiruppadiyam hymn
beginning with the expression [kōla]naṟkuḻal composed (?) by the donor's father
Kāyāvāṉ Arunilai, on the seventh day of the annual festival in the temple.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] kāntaḷūrcālai kalamaṟutta-
kōrājarājakēsaripanma[ṟ*]kku yāṇṭu 10- ttāvatu tāma••••••

iṭomākil

inscription.head:
No. 334.
(A.R. No. 334 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJĀDHIRĀJA (I)—30th YEAR (A.D. 1047-48).
inscription.p:
This records an agreement given by the servants of the temple of
Perumānaḍigaḷ at Gōvindapāḍi (here stated to be situated in Puriśai-nāḍu
(not Valla-nāḍu), a division of Maṇaiyiṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻamaṇḍalam,
undertaking to meet the expenses of special offerings and worship on the day of
Puṇarvasu in Chittirai month every year, with the interest on 30 kāśu which
had been invested with them by a lady by name Pārvati-Sāni the wife of Pirāndūr
Nāgadōṇa-Kramavittaṉ of Āvipākkam alias Śōḻaśikhāmaṇi-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
in Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] kovirapāṇṭiyaṉ talaiyum cerala•••••••••
ntaṇṭāṟkoṇṭa kovi[rācakecaripanmarā]-

kanmika[ḷo]m kaḷattū[r*]kkoṭṭattu [ā]vipākkamākiya coḻacikāma-

ivvāḻvār kanmikaḷom cevi[ce]yyātoḻiyil patineṭṭu nāṭṭu

varkaḷe tanmātaṉamuḷḷiṭṭut tān veṇṭukovukku manṟiyum paṉṟupāṭiṟuppittum
ittaṉmam celuttuvikka[k ka*]ṭavarāka patineṭṭunāṭṭu śrī vayiṣṇavarkaḷ itu
patineṭṭu nāṭṭu śrī vayiṣṇavarkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 335.
(A.R. No. 335 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
RĀJARĀJA I—11th YEAR (A.D. 995-96)
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 96 sheep by one Māyileṭṭi Veṇkāḍaṉ of Paṭṭam in
Āvūr-kūṟṟam in Śoḻanāḍu for burning a perpetual lamp before the image of
Anumadēva (Hanūmān) set up by him. The donor is also stated to have
endowed 10 kaḻañju of gold left in charge of the Ūrār of Vēḷkaḷa Veṇpākkam to
provide for the daily offerings to the same deity with 2 nāḻi of rice.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] kāntaḷūrccālai ka[lamaṟut]ta ko-
rājarājakēsarivanmaṟku yāṇṭu [1]1- tu

l.ta: yār pakkalk koṇṭu kaṭava poṉ tan

inscription.head:
No. 336.
(A.R. No. 336 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
The writing of the inscription seems to be of about the 11th century A.D.
It is built in at the beginning. It records an endowment of 25 kaḻañju of gold
by a person by name Brahmaśrī Toṅgarājaṉ who left it incharge of Padmanābha-
yōgi the manager of the temple at Gōvindapāḍi for the daily feeding of a
Brāhmaṇa in the temple with the interest thereon.

l.ta:
jaṉ śrī kovintapāṭi niṉṟaruḷiya
perumāṉaṭikaḷukku ekā

ṇanukku vaiytta poṉ eṭarpuram niṟai
irupattaiṅkaḻañcu poṉṉāl vanta poliyūṭṭukkoṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 337.
(A.R. No. 337 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARAK. RĀJĒNDRA-CHŌḶADĒVA I—3rd YEAR (A.D. 1014-15)
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged. It seems to record an endowment of
21 kaḻañju of gold to the temple at Gōvindapāḍi in Vallanāḍu by Kōḍaṉ Nambaṉ
[Na]ttagaḍi alias Uttamaśōḻa Brahma-Vaḷavarājaṉ of Aruvēḷūr alias Vikrama-
[śiṅga]-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Nenmali-nāḍu a sub-division of Arumoḻidēva-
vaḷanāḍu in Śōḻamaṇḍalam for the feeding of Śrīvaishṇavas on all the 9 days
of the festival in Chittirai month in the temple. With this amount the temple
is stated to have purchased tax-free, a piece of land from the Sabhā of
Arumoḻidēva-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Valla-nādu. Mention is made at the end
of the record, of the Sabhā of Parakulāntaka-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, the relevency
of which is not clear.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] kopparakeśaripanmarāna [śrī] irājentracoḻatevaṟku
[y]āṇṭu 3- āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu tāmarkoṭṭattu vallanāṭṭu
śrī kovintapāṭi ṉiṉṟaruḷiya perumāṉaṭikaḷ āḻvār cittirait-

tiruviḻā oṉpatu [nā]ḷum śrī vaiyiṣṇavarai••••••••• [perun]

inscription.head:
No. 338.
(A.R. No. 338 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN—Date lost.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XIX, No. 445

inscription.head:
No. 339.
(A.R. No. 339 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.

PARAKĒSARIVARMAN—Date lost. [10 ?]

inscription.head:
No. 340.
(A.R. No. 340 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE WEST AND SOUTH
WALLS OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJARĀJA I—20th YEAR (A.D. 1004-05)
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged. It seems to register a sale of land about
947 kuḻi in extent, by the Ūrār of Amaṇpākkam to the temple at Gōvindapāḍi.
It is stated that the cost of the land was paid by Ponvilli alias Vaishṇavadāsan,
a resident of Kuṇḍūr who formed a flower garden out of it for god Śrī Krishṇa.
The land was made tax-free by the Ūrār with the amount which had been
deposited with them sometime previously.

edition.l:
svasti śrī || tirumakaḷpolap peruṉilac celviyuṉ taṉakke yurimai
pūṇṭamai manakkoḷak kāntaḷurccālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi

inscription.head:
No. 341.
(A.R. No. 341 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALLS.
PARĀNTAKA I—39th YEAR (A.D. 945-46)
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Āṉi, śu. 5, Mūlā, Friday which would
correspond to A.D. 946.

This registers a sale on this date of 12 paṭṭi of tax-free garden land at
Piḍāriyār kuḷattūr belonging to Kaṟṟaḷi Māyileṭṭi Nīlakaṇṭaṉ, a merchant of
Koṟṟamāvalampāḍi in Kachchippēḍu (Kāñchīpuram) to the temple of Parama-
svāmi at Gōvindapāḍi in Valla-nāḍu, a division of Dāmarkōṭṭam. Among the
boundaries of the land is mentioned a river called the Pāriyāṟu after a well-
known cheiftain of the Śaṅgam period. It is probably the same as Pālār.

edition.l: svastiśrī ||—

l.ta:
ku yāṇṭu muppat
tonpatāvatu yāṇṭu ānittiṅkaḷ muṉṉāṭpakkam pañcamināḷ mūlaṅkiḻamai
veḷḷi poḻtu naṉpakal muṉkoṟkuṟi ikkovukkitu yāṇṭu itu tiṅkaḷ itu
nāḷitu poḻtu itu pa

vaṇakkaḷ varoṅ keṭṭa nilavilaiyāvaṇam kaccip
peṭṭu koṟṟamāvalāṉpāṭiperu-

ñcāmi vāṇiyaṉ kaṟṟaḷi māyileṭṭi nilakaṇṭaṉeṉ yāṉ piṭāriyār
kuḷattūr yāṉuṭaiya māntoṭṭam iṟaiyiliyāṉa paṉṉiraṇṭu paṭṭi ñilamum
viṟṟukkuṭuppataṟkellai kiḻpāṟkellai pā[ri]yāṟṟiṉ meṟkun teṉpāṟkellai
muttār pom peruvatiyiṉ vaṭa

kum vaṭapāṟkellai paṉaṅkāṭṭukkup pāynta uṭciṟukāliṉukkum icceṟuviṉ
teṉ varampiṉ teṟkumāka innā-

ṉkellai akattuñ cuṭṭappaṭṭa nilam uṇṇila moḻiviṉṟi viṟṟuk

niṉṟaruḷiṉa parama

kuṭutteṉ
kaṟṟaḷi māyilaṭṭiyāṉa nilakaṇṭaneṉ itu yirukkālāvatum mukkālāvatum

kiṇaṟukaḷum puṟṟum teṟṟikaḷum payatimaramu[m*] teṅkum pilāvu māvuṅkaḷavum

paṭṭiyum nāṉuṭaiya parice iṟaiyiliyāka vilaikkāṇaṅ kiḻikait tāṟṟi kiḻikaik
koṇṭu viṟṟu

kuṭutteṉ muṟcollappaṭṭa peruñcāmi vāṇiya

ṇaṅ kaiyttāṟṟi vilaipporuḷaṟak kuṭuttu viṟṟukkoṇṭār kaṟṟaḷi

pāṭi niṉṟaruḷiṉa para

ṉeḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 342.
(A.R. No. 342 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH, WEST AND SOUTH WALLS OF
THE CENTRAL SHRINE.
RĀJARĀJA I—23rd YEAR (A.D. 1007-08)
inscription.p:
This has the introduction Tirumagaḷpōla etc. It records an endowment of
90 kaḻañju of gold invested with the Ūrār of Vēḻkaḷa Veṇpākkam a dēvadāna
village of Gōvindapāḍi-Dēvar by Kāmaṉ Kōmaṇḍai a native of Ēḻūr-Amaṇai in
Venbaikkuḍi-nāḍu a division of Pāṇḍi-nāḍu also called Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, with
the condition that they were to measure out every year, as interest on the
amount, 120 kalam of paddy in the temple, for the (daily) offering of akkāra-
dalai preparation to the God. The donor also presented 15 cows to the temple
for the supply of milk as ingredient to the offering.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tirumakaḷpolap perunilaccelviyun taṉakke yurimai
pūṇṭamai maṉakkoḷak kāntaḷūrccālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi v[eṅkaināṭuṅ kaṅka]pāṭiyu
nuḷampapāṭiyun taṭikaipāṭiyuṅ kuṭamalaināṭuṅ kollamuṅ kaliṅkamumeṇṭicai pukaḻtara
īḻamaṇṭalamum i[ra*]ṭṭapāṭi eḻarai ilakkamu ntiṇṭiṟal veṉṟit taṇṭāṟ
koṇṭa taṉṉeḻil vaḷarūḻi ellāyāṇṭun toḻutakai viḷaṅkum yāṇṭe
ceḻiyarait tecukoḷ śrī kōrājarāja rājakēsariparmarāṉa śrī rājarājadēvaṟku
yāṇṭu irupattumuṉṟāvatu tāmarkoṭṭattut tāmarnāṭṭu śrī kovindapāṭit tevar
tevatāṉam veḻkaḷa veṇpākkattūrom kai-

yeḻuttu pāṇṭināṭāṉa rājarājavaḷanāṭṭu veṇpaikkuṭināṭṭu eḻūr
amaṇaiyuṭaiyāṉ kāmaṉ komaṇṭai kaiyāl yāṅkaḷ koṇṭu kaṭava poṉ

kaṭṭaḷait tuḷainiṟai toṇṇūṟṟuk kaḻañcu poṉ koṇṭu kaṭavom ippoṉ
toṇṇūṟṟuk kaḻañcu

pāṭi emperumāṉukku vaitta akkāra
ṭalaikku niṉṟāṉāṉa marakkālāl āṇṭuvarai poṉ kaḻañcukku poliyūṭṭuneṟ
kalaṉetūṇiyāka ittoṇṇūṟṟuk kaḻañcukkum vanta nel ṉūṟṟirupatiṉ kalamum
pākkūli paṭṭu

aḷantukuṭuppomāṉom innel
nūṟṟirupatiṉ kalattukkum ūrom paṭṭa pāk-

ceytukuṭuttom veṉkaḷa veṇpākkattūrom ūrom colla eḻuti
ṉeṉ ivvūr

ṉ tappi taṇṇintuḻāyeṉ ivai eṉ eḻuttu ikkom
maṇṭai vaicca akkāraṭalaikku pālamutu
ku ivaṉ vacca pacu patiṉaiñcu i

inscription.head:
No. 343.
(A.R. No. 343 of 1906)
NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, WALAJAPET TALUK, TIRUMĀLPURAM.
RUINED VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALLS.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN—8th YEAR

inscription.head:
No. 347.
(A.R. No. 347 of 1906)
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, ŪTTUKKĀḌU.
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR THE LOCAL TANK BUND.
PARĀNTAKA I—32nd YEAR (A.D. 938-39)
inscription.p:
The inscription states that this tank was dug by Oṟṟiyūr-Perumāṉ son of
Narumāñjirai (?) Kaliyoṭṭi of Ūṟṟukkāḍu a division of Ūṟṟukkāṭṭu-kōṭṭam.

inscription.head:
No. 348.
(A.R. No. 348 of 1906)
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, ŪTTUKKĀḌU.
ON THE SAME STONE (REVERSE).
inscription.p:
The first two lines are in early Telugu script, the rest being in Tamil.
The inscription is damaged. It seems to record the offering by a man by name
Iravi Achchal Bavadaḻi (?) of his own head in sacrifice as propitiation for the
successful completion of the tank. To the right of the epigraph is the squatting
figure of a man with his left hand resting on his waist and the right cutting off
his own head with a sword. From the top of the severed head—curiously—
instead of from the neck, is gushing forth a stream of blood falling on his right
side in the fashion of Gaṇgā flowing from Śivā's head.

inscription.head:
No. 349.
(A.R. No. 349 of 1906)
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, ŪTTUKKĀḌU.
ON THE RIGHT SIDE OF THE STEPS LEADING
TO THE TANK
inscription.p: This is dated in Śaka 1598, Nala and is in Telugu script.

l.tetr:
Śrī Rāma Svasti śrī vijay-ābhyudaya śālivāhana śaka-varshaṁbulu 1598
agu nēṭi Naḷanāma saṁvatsara Phālguna śuddha 15 Śukravāraṁ
nāḍu Tirumala Tōḷa-

ppāchāryulayyavāri priya śishyulaina paṁtta-vaṁśa pāvamulaina
Madhurāṁtaka gōtrajulaina śrī Pēṭanārā-reḍḍi komāruṁḍu Yallā-
reḍḍikinni Liṁggamakkakunnu janmiṁchchina

Kṛishṇā-reḍḍi ā Kṛishṇā-reḍḍiki Yimmaḍakkaku janmiṁchchina Yellā-
reḍḍi ā Yallā-reḍḍiki Yīśvaravāka-gōtraṁ Dēvā-reḍḍi Nāgā-reḍḍi
komārtte Akkamaku janmiṁchchina Ayyapa-reḍḍi Kṛishṇā-reḍḍi

Perā-reḍḍi Nāgā-reḍḍi Gaṁggā-reḍḍi Raṁgappa reḍḍi yī ārugurilō
śrīmad-anaṁtta-kalyāṇa paripūrṇulaina rāja-śrī Pēṭa Gaṁggā-
reḍḍigāru Goṁdurupāla gōtraṁ Duharikāḷā-reḍḍi ko-

mārtte tana priya bhārya Tulaśammatō kūḍa Kāṁchchi-nagarānnaku
tūrpuna uṁḍḍē Ūṭukōṭalō Manutīrthānaku pratināmamaina
Bhairava-koḷaṁ jīrṇṇōddhāraṇaṁ chēśi chuṭṭu rātikaṭṭa[ḍa*]mu

tūrpu dakshiṇāla saupānamulunnu kaṭṭiṁchchina taṭāka pratishṭha
dharmamu āchaṁdrārka sthāyigā neravērchchina Pēṭa Gaṁggā-
reḍḍigāru annalu tammulu ārgurunnu Yiśvaravāka-gōtrajuḍaina
Dēvā-reḍḍi

Nāgā-reḍḍikini Pēṭa Yallā-reḍḍi komārtte Akkamaku janmiṁchchina
tama mēnamāmalu Peda Ayyapa-reḍḍi Reḍḍipa-reḍḍi Channā-reḍḍi
Pōlā-reḍḍi China Ayyapa-reḍḍi Gaṁggā-reḍḍigāru ārgurunnu kū-

ḍā Dēsūri-sthalāna nūtanaṁggā kōṭa peṭṭiṁchchi Dēsūrivāru anēṭaṭṭugā
prasiddhik-ekki sapta-saṁttān-ādi dharmamulunnu neravērchchi
nānā-dikkula yaṁddu kīrti pratāpaṁ kaluga naḍichināru yī-

taṭāka pratishṭha chēśina Pēṭa Gaṁggā-reḍḍigāru tama komāḷḷu Rāmā-
reḍḍi Achārla-reḍḍi Nāgā-reḍḍi Kṛishṇā-reḍḍigāritō kūḍā Śrī Rāma
kṛipā kaṭākshaṁvalla sakala bhāgyamulu dhīrghāyussu kaligē
sukhāna vaṁdduru gāka Śrī Śrī•

inscription.head:
No. 350.
(A.R. No. 350 of 1906)
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, ŪTTUKKĀḌU.
ON A STONE SET UP IN A FIELD NEAR THE VILLAGE.
inscription.p:
This is in modern characters and is dated in the (cyclic) year Prajōtpati.
Panguni 15. It records an endowment of 200 kuḻi of land by a certain
Raghupati. Nāyakkar for worship of goddess Kāmākshi-Ammai. On the top of
the stone is figured a lion facing the proper right.

inscription.head:
No. 351.
(A.R. No. 351 of 1906)
CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT, KANCHIPURAM TALUK, ŪTTUKKĀḌU.
ON A STONE SET UP NEAR THE KANNĀR MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
This is also in modern characters and is indifferently worded. It is dated
in Hēviḷambi, Vaigāśi, 15, Thursday, and records the formation of a flower
garden on 200 kuḻi of land at Kannār to the south-east of Vairavan-kēṇi by one
Ellappa Reḍḍi (?) with the help of Saiyid Vibaramu (Ibrahim) Sāyabu and
Venkaṭādri-Mudaliār the Pārapatyam of Vallappakkam and also the residents of
the village.

inscription.head:
No. 352.
(A.R. No. 352 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE
AKHILĀṆḌĒŚVARĪ SHRINE.
TRIBH. VĪRA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA—13th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is damaged. The details of the date are given as Mēsha, śu.
Sunday, Hastā probably corresponding to A.D. 1266, April 18. The tithī
should be śu. 13. The epigraph states that as the temple at Tirunalakkuṉṟam
(Kuḍumiyāmalai) in Kō-nāḍu had gone out of repairs and there was no money
for its renovation, the Nāṭṭar of Kāna-nāḍu and those connected with the temple
administration sold a land called the Iluppaikkuḍi-vayal at Ammaṅgainallūr,
which was a tirunāmattukkāṇi of the temple, to a certain dēvaraḍiyāḷ of the
temple by name Nāchchiyār daughter of Durggaiyāṇḍāḷ for 73300 kāśu. Kāna-
nāḍu is said to be situated in Kuṉṟuśūḻ-nāḍu a division of Kō-nāḍu also called
Kaḍalāḍaiyādilaṅgaikoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 353.
(A.R. No. 353 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. VĪRA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA—13th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is connected with No. 352 above, and registers the endorsement
(kīḻpuṉaitīṭṭu) of the sale of the land and acknowledgement of the receipt of the
amount from the purchaser Nāchchīyār by the nāṭṭār of Kōṉāḍu and the temple
authorities of Tirunalakkuṉṟam.

ṭaiyātilaṅkaikoṇṭa coḻavaḷanāṭṭu nāṭṭomum ikkoyiṟ tevar
kaṉmi koyiṟkaṇakkaromum ivvaṉaivom ivvūrt tevaraṭiyāril tukkai
yāṇṭār makaḷ nācci-

caṇṭe

inscription.head:
No. 354.
(A.R. No. 354 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE
AKHILĀṆḌĒSVARĪ SHRINE.
MĀR. SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA I—14th YEAR (A.D. 1229-30).
inscription.p:
This begins with the introduction Pūmaruviya Tirumaḍandaiyum etc. It
registers a gift of two (adjoining) pieces of land at Śikaranallūr in Kuṉṟiyūr-
nāḍu on the east bank of the river Veḷḷāṟu as tiruvēṭṭai-tiruttōppu to the temple
of Tirunalakkuṉṟamuḍaiya-Nāyanār by Śaṅkaraṉ Kaṇḍaṉ alias Kalikaḍinda-
Pāṇḍyadēvaṉ of Kuḷattūr in Malai-maṇḍalam after purchasing the same from
some specified individuals, residents of the place. The purpose of the gift is
evidently to provide a garden where the image of the god was taken in procession
and worshipped on certain festival occasions as will be seen by No. 355 below.

veṉṟaḻaittu vānilattulaiya va-

ntirukka ponni vaḷavaṉ(vaṉ) urimaiyoṭum pukuttu peṟṟa mutal
vaṇṇaniṉ poreṉṟu muṉkaviṭuvaṟṟi ariyaṇaikiḻ viḻuntu toḻutu•••••••
kaikalanta•••••••• ttāṇṭār raviyum m[e]ṉi viṭṭakala•• tarumārveḷai
nittiyam viṭṭapaṭik koṉṟaṅkitu piṭipāṭākaveṉap poṅku vicai cālattu muyarvā-

•••• viḷaṅka••••••••• ṉṟun tirumukamum paṇṭiḻanta ceraṉpati
eṉṉu nāmamun ceraṉ kāruvam miḷa uḻaṅki viṭai kuṭuttuviṭṭaruḷi vākaitti••••
vaṭa[ve]ntartam pātam montapaṇiya virakkaḻallaṇintu viḷaṅkiya maṇiyaṇi vira
ciṅṅātanattu vaṉaṅkeḻukavar irumaruṅkicaippa ka[viṇi]yar muḻuṅku kaḷaṅkaliliyāṉai
vempulattu

tirumaṇippuyam piriyā ilaṅku kulam arivaiyar toḻutu [māṉilattu]
ulakamuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya māmutal matikkulam viḷaṅkiya komutal
komāṟu panmarāṉa tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ coṇāṭu vaḻaṅkiyaruḷiya cuntarapāṇṭiya
tevaṟku yāṇṭu patinālāvatu malaimaṇṭalattu kuḷattūr caṅkaran kaṇṭanāṉa-

kalikaṭinta pāṇṭiya tevanen kuṉṟiyūrṉāṭṭu cikaranallūr catti nampiyāna
tampirān toḻaṉum tevar kotaṇṭavam perumāḷ perumāṉum perumāḷ nampiyum
utaiyan perumāḷum āccampi kotaṇṭan uḷḷiṭṭārum ventan uṭaiyāṉum
ivvaṉaivarpakkal nāṉ vilaikoṇṭuṭaiya veḷḷā-

[ṟṟu]kku kiḻkkaraiyil arai mā ceynilamum maṇittoṭṭamum ivarkaḷ
pakkal nāṉ vilaikoṇṭuṭaiya pāṭa[ka]ttil peruṉānkellaiyāvaṉa kiḻpāṟkellai
peruvaḻiyil nāṭṭina tiruccūlakkal[lukku] meṟku teṉpāṟkellai kunṟaṉ
pirāntakateva[ṉ*] uḷḷiṭṭār araiyar toṭṭattukku vaṭaveliyil nā-

ṭṭina tiruccūlakkallukku vaṭakku melpāṟkellai veḷḷāṟṟukkuk
kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai ventar[maṉ]nan maṇaltoṭṭattu teṉtalaiyilālait
tiruccūlakkallukkut teṟkum āka icainta perunāṉkellaiyuḷ akappaṭṭa
innilam uṭaiya tirunalakkuṉṟamuṭaiya nāyanā[ṟ]kku tiruveṭṭai toppāka āti
taṇṭe-

edition.l:
curatevar cikatta[t*]tile nirvārttu kalveṭṭikkuṭutten caṅkaraṉ
kaṇṭanāna kalikaṭinta pāṇṭiya tevaneṉ ivaiyen eḻuttu ||—ivai paṉmāhēśvara
rakṣai ||—

inscription.head:
No. 355.
(A.R. No. 355 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. (MĀR.) SUNDARAPĀṆḌYA I—14th Year (A.D. 1229-30).
inscription.p:
The king is given the attribute “who presented the Chōḷa country” as in
the inscription above. This records a further gift made by the same donor
Śaṅkaraṉ Kaṇḍaṉ providing by means of an agreement with the nāṭṭār for the
supply of 10 kalam and 5 kuṟaṇi of paddy every year by the nāṭṭār of Malai-
nāḍu (probably by some investment with them), for the offering of Tiruppāvāḍai-
amudu to the deity after being taken in procession to the garden (Tiruvēṭṭait-
tōppu) called the Tyāgañjiriyān-tiruttōppu on the respective days of festival in the
months of Mārgaḻi, Māśi and Paṅguni.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ coṇāṭu vaḻaṅkiyaruḷiya śrī cuntara
pāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu patinālāvatu malaimaṇṭalattu kuḷattūr caṅkaraṉ
kaṇṭaṉāṉa kalikaṭinta pāṇṭiya tevaṉeṉ uṭaiyār tirunalakkuṉṟamuṭaiya
nāyaṉāṟku tiruveṭṭai toppāka veḷḷāṟṟuk kiḻkaraiyil

l.ta:
cikaranallūrpaṟṟil ivvūr tampirāntoḻaṉ uḷḷiṭṭārpakkalum āccampi
kotaṇṭaṉ uḷḷiṭṭār pakkalum ṉān vilaikoṇṭu kuṭutta tiyākañciriyāṉ
tiruveṭṭaittoppil uṭaiyār tirunalakkuṉṟamuṭaiyaṉāyaṉār tiruṉāḷ muṉṟilum
tiruveṭṭai eḻuntaruḷi tiruppāvāṭai-

amutu ceytaruḷa malainnāṭṭu nāṭṭārpakkal upaiyattukku nāṉ
koṇṭa piramāṇappaṭiye••••••••• nellup patinkalane aiṅkuṟuṇiyum
tirunāḷ oṉṟukku tiruveṭṭai eḻuntaruḷi tiruppāvāṭai amutu ceytaruḷa
tiruvamutarici kalamāka tiruṉāḷ muṉṟukkut tiruvamutarici

mukkalattukkum iraṇṭañcāka nel eḻukalane tūṇippatakkuṅ kaṟiyamutuṭ
paṭṭa pala viñcaṉaṅkaḷukku nellu irukalane irutūṇimukkuṟuṇi[yu]māka nellup
patinkalane a[y*]kuṟuṇiyum patinālāvatu tirumārkaḻi tirunāḷukkun tirumācit
tirunāḷukkun tiru[ppaṅku]nittiruṉāḷukkum viḷaiyilum viḷaiyāviṭi-

lun tiyākañciriyān tiruttoppil cerapāṇṭiyan tirumaṇṭapatte
aḷakkakkaṭavārkaḷāka eṉakkavarkaḷeḻutittanta piramāṇappaṭiye cantirātittatavaṟ
celvatāka ātitaṇṭe

varatevar cikattattile nirvā[r*]ttukkuṭutteṉ caṅkaraṉ
kaṇṭanāṉa kalikaṭinta pāṇṭiya tevaṉeṉ ivai taiyūr nāṭṭu-

veḷāṉ eḻuttu ivai vayalakanāṭṭu veḷāṉ eḻuttu ivai teṉ
k[o]ṉāṭṭu veḷāṉ eḻuttu ivai amaraṉ uṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu ivai tiruñāṉa
campantaṉ taṟkuṟiyātaṉmaikku taḻuvakkuḻaintāṉ muṟaippaṭippāṭuvāṉ eḻuttu
ivai aracu kaliyaṉ eḻuttu ivai [pū]pālaveḷāṉ eḻuttu ivai kuppaitevaṉ
taṟkuṟiyātaṉmaikku cem-

piyaṉ koṉāṭṭu veḷāṉ eḻuttu nāṭu paṇikka upaiyappiramāṇam
eḻutiṉeṉ nāṭṭukkaṇakkaril tukkiṉāṉ nakkaṉ eḻuttu itu paṉmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 356.
(A.R. No. 356 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
JAṬ. VĪRA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA—11th YEAR (A.D. 1263-64).
inscription.p:
This begins with introduction Tirumagaḷ vaḷar-mulai etc. It is left
incomplete. It registers a royal order issued at the request of (Minister)
Kāliṅgarāyar while the king was encamped at Karaiyūr in Kōnāḍu, making a
gift of the village Mēlmaṇanallūr together with all its dues and taxes like
kaḍamai, kaṟpūra-vilai etc, to the temple of Tirunalakkunram-uḍaiya-Nāyanār
to meet the expenses of worship and other requirements of goddess Tirukkāmak-
kōṭṭa-Nāchchiyār who had been consecrated, it is said, by Nāchchiyār the
daughter of Durggaiyāṇḍāḷ, evidently the same person mentioned in Nos. 352
and 353 above.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tirumakaḷ [vaḷa]rmulai tirumārpu taḷaippa porumaka[ḷ*] vaḷar
mulai puyampuṇarntu [ka]ḷippa naṉmoḻi nāṉmicaic coṉmakaḷiruppa [ti]caikaḷeṭ
ṭiṉum [ceyama]-

l.ta:
kaḷ vaḷara irumuṉṟu camaiyamum orumuṉṟu tamiḻum vetanāṉkum nitiyil
viḷaṅkak kaṅkaṅ kavuṭam ka[ṭā]raṅ kācipam koṅkaṅ kutiraṅ ko[llama] kutavam
arumaṉañ coṉakam ciṉa ma-

vanti karunaṭa miḻaṅ kaliṅkan teliṅkam pepaṉan taṇakam paṇṭara mutalirampuli
ventaru mikal maṇṭalikarum mummuraicu muḻaṅkum cemmaṇi māḷikai koyiṟ
ko[ṟ*]ṟa vāyiṟ pukuntu kāla[m]

pār[t*]tu kaḻaliṇai paṇintu [na]lveḻamum netiyamum kāṭṭi pūviricolai
kāvikaḷattu coḻaṉ poruta [v]eḻapporil matappiṟṟāṟāka katakkaḷiyāṉai tuḷaikkac
cempoṟṟoṭikkaiyiṟ-

(ṟoṭikkaiyiṟ) piṭittu vaḷaittu melkoṇṭu vākaicūṭi talaip
perāṇmai taṉitaṉi yeṭuttu talai[kka]vinaracar kaviṉpeṟattutippateṟṟamaṉṉar
[ttātati]yāmal oṟṟaiyāḻiyulaku valamara e-

ṉai ma[ṉ*]ṉavar teṟkoṭiṟaiñca miṉavar koṭit teruvileṅka [vacu]
varaik koṭuṅkol vaḻaṅkā vaṇmanantalai ceṅkol nāṭoṟu naṭappa etticai
maṉṉarum miruṅkali kaṭintu muttave-

ṇkuṭai muḻunilavu coriya orumoḻi tirappap puva[na*]muḻu[tum*] taṉ
matamārpu viḷaṅka maṇimuṭi cūṭi uraikeḻuppa pala araiciyal vaḻakkam neṟippaṭa
nāṭṭuṅ kuṟippiṉuḷḷicaintiruppa tañce[lavat]tiru-

nta mantiri [cara]ṇmai tikaḻntiṉitu nokki muraṇmiku ciṟappil īḻa[mu]
maṉṉar ilakuvariloruvaṉai viḻa [mu]ṭporutu viṇmicaiyeṟṟi urimaiccuṟṟamum
uya[r*]kulam pukuta taṉmai yāṇmaiyum pala-

paṭaippuraviyum kaṉamaṇitterum ciṉavaṭamarum kā[na]kattorum navamaṇik
kuvaiyum āṭakattiriyum ariācaṉamum muṭiyum kaṭakamum muḻumaṇiyāramum koṭiyuṅ
kuṭaiyum kuḷirveṇkavariyum muracuñ caṅ-

kamum [taṉamu] mutali araicu keḻutāya maṭaiyavar kāṇā maṉnavar kaṇṭu
kaṇṭeṅka koṇāmalaiyaṉu[m] tirikūṭakiriyāṉum urukeḻu koṭimicai irukayaleḻuti
eṉai ventaṉai āṉaitiṟaikoṇṭu

[paṇ]ṭeval ceyyātikal ceytirunta cāvaṉmaintaṉ nala mi[ku]n
tiṟaiñca virakkaḻal li[ru]varaic caṭṭat tirukkoḷam alaivarap paṭaṉ kaḻittu vaḻaṅki
aruḷi muḻaṅku kaḷiṟeṟi pārmuḻutaṟiya ūrva-

lañ[ce][y*] vit[tu na]ntai āṇṭa taṭaṅkaṭaliḻam maintaṉ pe[ṭik]ku
marapeṉa niṉaippiṭṭaraiciṭa maki[ḻ*]ntu āṉūr [a]ḷiccuviraiyaccelkeṉṟurai
kuṭuttaruḷi yākamaṭantai aṉpuṭaṉ cātti vākai cūṭi ma-

tumaṇaṅ kama[ḻ*] malarnta veṇkavariṉ vāṭalun teṉṟalum ventarvica
viraciṅkātaṉattu ka[ṟ]panantaḻuviya kāmarval-

••• ta poṟṟoṭi puṉaintu malarnta malarkkoḻum paripuraic ciṟṟaṭi
ulakamuḻutumuṭaiyāroṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya

edition.l:
ccaṭaipaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ ciri virapāṇṭiya tevaṟku
yāṇṭu 11- vatu ḷ 103- ṉāl koṉāṭṭukkāraiyūril eḻuntaruḷi irukka iṉṉāṭṭut
tirunalakkuṉṟattu uṭaiyār tirunalakkuṉ-

l.ta:
ṟamuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil tāṉattār kaṇṭu ikkoyiṟ tirumeṟṟaḷi
yuṭaiya makātevaṟku ivūrt tevaraṭiyāril tukkaiyāṇ[ṭi]ṭāḷ makaḷ nācci
eḻuntaruḷivitta tirukkāmakkoṭṭattu nācciyār uruvu-

ṭai[mu]lai m(ā)ṅkaiyārkkup pūcaikkum tiruppaṇikkum veṇṭum nimantaṅ
kaḷukkum uṭalāka iṉnāṭṭu melmaṇanallūr nāṉkellai uḷḷakappaṭṭa kuḷamum
kuḷapparippum nattamu[m*] nattappāḻum

naṉce[y*] puṉcey toṭṭamum uṭpaṭṭa nilattil i[v*]vūr
tiruvikkirama coḷi

ramuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku kuṭikkāṭu akavayal uṭpaṭa nilam araiyum
tirunalakkuṉṟamuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil nāṟpatteṇṇāyi-

ravaṉ tirumaṭattukku maṭappuṟam nilam araiyum āka nilam veli nikki
nilattil paḻantevatāṉam nikki nikki uḷḷa nilattuk kaṭamai kaṟpūravilaik kāriyavā
rāṭci pañcupili cantu

ṭṭārppāṭṭam iṉavari īḻampuñcey cekkiṟai poṉvari ilāñciṉaip
peṟu antarāyam ataraviṉiyokam accuvari veṭṭippāṭṭam iṭaivari maṟṟum
epperpaṭṭaṉavum uṭpaṭap patiṉoṉṟāvatu

edition.l:
mutal tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliāka iṭappeṟaveṇumeṉṟu k[ā]liṅkarāyar
namakkuc coṉṉamaiyil ittirunalakkuṉṟamuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyil tirumelttaḷi
yuṭaiya makātevarkku i[v*]vūr
ṣtops here incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 357.
(A.R. No. 357 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL
OF THE SECOND PRĀKĀRA.
TRIB. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶADĒVA—18th YEAR
inscription.p:
This registers a gift of puravu land, two mā in extent, at Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam by Kaṉṉaṉ Edirilipperumāḷ alias Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Kaḍamba-
rāyaṉ of Uṟattūrkūṟṟam in Kuṉṟuśūḻ-nāḍu stated to be a division of Iraṭṭapāḍi-
koṇḍa-vaḷanāḍu, to one Bālāśrayan Śiṅgaṉ Śiṅgaṉ alias Nāṟpatteṉṉāyira-nambi
of Ādanūr, who was to provide offerings to the goddess Periya-Nāchchiyār during
the early morning service in the temple at Tirunalakkuṉṟam in the same nāḍu.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī kulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku
yāṇṭu 18- vatu nāḷ 195- l iraṭṭapāṭi[koṇṭa]vaḷanāṭṭuk kuṉṟucūḻnāṭṭu
uṭaiyār tirunalakkuṉṟamuṭaiya nā-

l.ta:
yaṉār koyiṟ periyanācciyārkkut tiruppaḷḷi eḻucci cantikkut tiru
amutupaṭikkum kaṟiamutu uḷḷiṭṭa viñcaṉaṅkaḷukkum patitta patipattamāka cantirā
ā

ttūrkūṟṟattuc cāra araiyaṉ [ka]ṉṉaṉ etirilipperumāḷāṉa kulot
tuṅkacoḻak kaṭamparāyaṉeṉ ikkūṟṟattu śrī teva[kaḷ t]evaṉṉāṉa aḻakiya
maṇavāḷac caruppetimaṅkalattup pura-

viṉpaṭi nilattil ātaṉūr pālāḻrayaṉ ciṅkaṉ ciṅkaṉ tiruvaraṅkapāḷi
yāṉa nāṟpatteṇṇāyiranampi nāḷippaṟṟāṉa vaṭakkil nila[m*] kuṭiniṅkāt
tevatāṉam kiḻakkuvayal nilam pa piṭākai putuvayal

pa iṉṉilam iraṇṭu māvum tiruccūlakkallu [n]āṭṭu
vittu ivvūr aracai puṭam i[ṭṭa] kuṭimai paṟṟu maṟṟa epperpaṭṭatuvum nāyaṉārkku
nāṟpatteṇṇāyiranampiyeṉ cantirā

edition.l:
kalveṭṭikkuṭutteṉ kulottuṅkacoḻak kaṭamparāyaṉeṉ ivai
kulottuṅkacoḻak kaṭamparāyaṉ eḻuttu ivai nampiyāṇṭāṉ eḻuttu ivai
paṉmāyesva[ra*]raṣai ||—

inscription.head:
No. 358.
(A.R. No. 358 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN—[8]th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XIX, No. 198.

inscription.head:
No. 359.
(A.R. No. 359 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶADĒVA—15th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of 10 mā of land as kuḍinīṅgā-dēvadānam at Viśalūr in
Aṇṇavāyil-kūṟṟam, after purchasing the same from the Ūrār of the place, by
Nāchchi Malaiyāḻvi daughter (?) of Periyanāṭṭāchāriyaṉ of Tirukkoḍuṅguṉṟam
to provide for the supply of 60 kalam of paddy every year at 6 kalam per mā
of land, to the temple at Tirunalakkuṉṟam.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ kulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu
15- vatu iraṭṭap[āṭi]koṇṭa coḻavaḷanāṭṭuk kuṉṟicūḻnāṭṭut tirunalak
kuṉṟattu•••• tat tirukkoṭuṅkuṉṟam uṭai-

l.ta:
yā[n]āṉa pe[ri]yanāṭṭācāriyaṉ maka[ḷ] nāccimalaiyāḻviyeṉ uṭai
yār tirunalakkuṉṟamuṭaiya nāyaṉārkkuk kuṭiniṅkāt tevatāṉamāy nāṉ kalveṭṭik
kuṭutta paricāvatu nāṉ iṉṉāṭṭu aṇṇavāyil

kūṟṟattu vicalūr ūrārpakkal vilaikoṇṭu uṭaiyeṉāy eṉṉutāṉa
alaivayalukkuk kiḻpāṟkellai [k]āṭaṉ kovintaṉ uḷḷiṭṭār pāliyūraṉ
ceykkum maṉattuḷāṭuvāṉ aracu kovintaṉ paṉma-

ṉuḷḷiṭṭār ta[la]ṅkaḷ eriyāṉceykku[m me]ṟkum tenpāṟkellai
vicalūr menilakkuḷattukku nirpoṉavatikku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai cikkanallūr
ellaikkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai muṟkoṭikaḷamānacey neṭumpāṭṭu-

kkum avani nārāyaṇa muventira coḻaveḷāṉ civaṉ puṉceykkut
teṟkum ākap perunāṉkellaikkuḷpaṭṭa nilattu naṉcai viḷainilam pattu
mā[vu*]kkum iṉṉāyanāṟku iṟaiy i[ṟu]ttu keraḷāntakaṉ marakkālāl māttāl
aṟu-

kalamāka vanta nellu aḷakkakkaṭavārākavum iṉṉellu aḷakkumi[ṭa*]ttu
innāyaṉār tevatāṉam a[cal] iṟuttapaṭi iṟukkakaṭaveṉākavum antarāyam kiḻiṟai
uḷḷiṭṭa kuṭimaikaḷ acal iṟuttapaṭi iṟukkakkaṭaveṉākavum antarāya-

m kiḻiṟai uḷḷiṭṭa kuṭi[m*]aikaḷ tevatāṉam ceyyakkaṭaveṉākavum
innellu iṭṭu ikkoyilil aḻakiyacema[ṉ] tiruveṭuttukkaṭṭi kaṭṭi
āṭṭāṇṭutoṟum aḻivu certtu mikkutu śrī paṇṭāramāka āvatāka-

cammatittu kalveṭṭikkuṭutteṉ ikkoṭuṅkuṉṟamuṭaiyāṉāṉa pe[ri]ya
nāṭṭu ācāriyaṉ maka[ḷ] nāccimalaiyāḻviyeṉ itu paṉmā

inscription.head:
No. 360.
(A.R. No. 360 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF
THE SECOND PRĀKĀRA
JAṬ. VĪRAPĀṆḌYADĒVA—15th YEAR (A.D. 1267—68)
inscription.p:
The king is given the attribute “who took Īḻam, Koṅgu and Chōḷa-
maṇḍalam and after conquering the powerful king was pleased to perform the
anointment of heroes and victors at Perumbaṟṟappuliyūr”. The inscription
registers a sale, as tirunāmattukkāṇi to the temple at Tirunalakkuṉṟam, of three
vēli of puravu (tax-paying ?) land at Viśalūr by the Ūrār of that place, at the
instance of two officers of the king (Rājagōmuṟṟuvar) Vāṇādharāyar and Gāṅgēyar
and on the intercession of the Iraṇḍukarai-nāṭṭār, for 64,000 kāśu. The circum-
stances of the sale are given, from which it would seem that the Ūrār had been
at default for a long period in the payment of the kaḍamai-kīḻiṟai and had also
to account for 50,000 kāśu which had evidently been deposited with them out
of the amount intended for the renovation of the temple. This is probably the
sale-proceeds of the transaction recorded in No. 352. A supplementary sale deed
is added to this record figuring the Nāṭṭār as an agency and party for the
execution of the sale.

inscription.head:
No. 361.
(A.R. No. 361 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
JAṬ. VĪRA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA—15th YEAR.
inscription.p:
The king gets the same attributes as in No. 360 above. The inscription is
damaged. It records an agreement given by the nāṭṭār and the sthānattār of the
temple of Tirunalakkuṉṟamuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār, to a certain individual (name lost)
of Viśalūr, who had donated three mā of land to the temple, to the effect that
they would supply 2 kalam of rice and other requirements of the Tiruppāvāḍai-
amudu to the god on each of the four special festival days in the year, when
the deity was taken in procession to the temple garden. Incidental mention is
made of the conversion of a dēvadāna land at Viśalūr into a tirunāmattukkāṇi
of the temple by the Nāṭṭār and Ūrār, on which occasion this gift is said to
have been made by the donor.

inscription.head:
No. 362.
(A.R. No. 362 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
MĀR. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA I—3rd YEAR (A.D. 1218-19).
inscription.p:
The king claims the attribute “who was pleased to take the chōḷa
country”, even in this third regnal year. The inscription registers a public
sale of a piece of dry land at Śikaranallūr in Kuṉṟuśūḻ-nāḍu a division of
Teṉ-Kōṉāḍu evidently by the Ūrār of the place represented by a committee of
seven persons, and its purchase by Nambi Ponnambalakkūttaṉ alias Uḍaiyār
Sāṅgēyarayar of Āṟṟūr in Śōḻa-Pāṇḍya-vaḷanāḍu for 2(1/2) paḻaṅkāśu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ coṇāṭu
koṇṭu aruḷiya cuntarapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu 3- āvatu teṉkoṉāṭṭuk
kuṉṟucūḻnāṭṭuc cikaranallūr tiruvāḷaṉ coḻamuventaveḷāṉum tiruvuṭai-

l.ta:
y[ā*]n kotaṇṭaṉum cuntan koḻuntum cuntan kāriyum nampi
koḻuntum nampi paṉmanum poṉṉaṉ oṉṟāyiramuṭaiyāṉum ivvaṉaivom eṅkaḷ
kiḻai puṉcey vilaikkaṟa viṟkak koḷviruḷiroveṉṟu orukālāvatu mirukālāva-

tu mukkālāvatu muṟkkūṟap piṟkūṟi coḻapāṇṭiya vaḷanāṭṭu āṟṟū
ruṭaiyāṉ nampi poṉṉampalakkūttaṉāṉa uṭaiyār kāṅkeyarāyar koḷve
ṉeṉṟu aruḷicceyya muṉcollappaṭṭa ivvaṉaivarom nāṅkaḷ viṟkiṟa pu-

ṉceykkup perunānkellaiyāvaṉa ki[ḻ]pāṟkellai melpaṉainallūr
ūrppotu ellaikku [meṟku*]m teṉpāṟkellai tiruvāḷar co[ḻa]muventaveḷāṉ
ceykku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai cuntaṉkoḻuntum nampikoḻu-

ntum tiruvaṉ coḻaṉ muventaveḷāṉum nāṉkellaikku kiḻakkum

teṟkum āka icaintaperunāṉke-

llaikku uḷpaṭṭa puncey viṟṟukuṭuttu emmil icainta vilaipporuḷ
aṉṟāṭu naṟpaḻaṅkācu

nampipaṉ[ma]ṉum

inscription.head:
No. 363.
(A.R. No. 363 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA—8th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is a damaged inscription, which seems to register a sale of land
belonging to the temple of Tirunalakkuṉṟamuḍaiya-Nāyaṉār to a certain
Tāyilumnallā[ḷ] a woman servant (?) of Kulōttuṅga[sōḻa*]-Kaḍambarā[ya*]ṉ
of Uṟattūr-kūṟṟam. Tirunalakkuṉṟam is said to have been situated in Kuṉṟuśūḻ-
nāḍu a division of Iraṭṭapāḍikoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. The value of the land is
stated to be 50 kāśu.

svastiśrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī rājarāja tevaṟku yāṇṭu
8- vatu iraṭṭapāṭi koṇṭa coḻavaḷanāṭṭu kuṉṟicūḻnāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirunalak
kuṉṟamuṭaiya ṉāyaṉār dēvatāṉattu i-

kulottuṅka[coḻaka*]ṭamparā[ya*]ṉ aṇukki cunti tāyilumnallā[ḷ] caṇṭe

ḻpāṟk••••••••• pāṟkellai ūṟkku vaṭakku melpāṟkkellai

inscription.head:
No. 364.
(A.R. No. 364 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶADĒVA—35th YEAR
inscription.p:
The inscription is said to have been copied over in the 3rd year of
Sundara-Pāṇḍyadēva from its original position on the wall and tiers on the
north side of the davaṉa-maṇḍapa, as in its place a new maṇḍapa had to be
erected to enshrine the god Tirunaṭṭapperumāṉ. The order for this recopy is
said to have been issued by the officer (?) Poṉṉaṉ Gāṅgēyaṉ of Āṟṟūr the (the
same who figures in No 362 above), on Mārgaḻi, ba. 5, Saturday, Makhā
(= A.D. 1218, December 9. The day happens to be Sunday.)

This records an undertaking given by the Poṟkōyil-kaikkōḷa soldiers of the
three regiments of Kōnāḍu alias Iraṭṭapāḍikoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, to the temple at
Tirunalakkuṉṟam in Kuṉṟiśūḻ-nāḍu to collect among themselves every year a
specified quantity of paddy required for the several items of daily worship and
offerings to God Kaikkōḷa Nāyakar and his consort consecrated by them in the
temple, and an amount of 25 Kāśu for the expenses of the tiruppāvāḍai-amudu
offerings to the deities on the three occasions of festival in the temple in the
months of Mārgaḻi, Māśi and Paṅguṉi.

inscription.head:
No. 365.
(A.R. No. 365 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. VĪRA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA—7th YEAR
inscription.p:
This is an unfinished epigraph, evidently intended to register a sale of
land fed by three wells at Karandūr (village) belonging to Araśugaṇḍadēvaṉ
Araśagaḷañjappiṟandāṉ alias Ayilaiyarāyaṉ of Pulvayal (?) in Vayalaga-nāḍu a
division of Kōṉāḍu also called Kaḍalaḍaiyādilaṅgaikoṇḍa-śōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, to the
temple at Tirunalakkuṉṟam in Kuṉṟuśūḻ-nāḍu.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] tribhuva[nac]cakkaravattikaḷ śrī virapāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu

kāṇiyāṉa karantūr vayalil karantūṟappiyāṉa

inscription.head:
No. 366.
(A.R. No. 366 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
PARAKĒSARIVARMAN—10th YEAR.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XIX, No. 243.

inscription.head:
No. 367.
(A.R. No. 367 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ŚIKHĀNĀTHASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIB. RĀJARĀJADĒVA—5th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This records a gift of land as dēvadāna by a vēḷāṉ by name Kōdaṉdaṉ of
Śīkaranallūr in kuṉṟiśūḻ-nāḍu, a division of Raṭṭapāḍikoṇdaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, to
the temple of Trivikrama-Chōḷīśvaramuḍaiya-Mahādēva at Mēlmaṇinallūr, with
one Kōvaṉ Śōḻan as a permanent tenant paying the kuḍimai-kīḻiṟai thereon.

l.ta: tiripuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī rājarāja tevarkku yā[ṇ*]ṭu

ḷanāṭṭu kuṉṟicūḻnāṭṭu cikaranallūr veḷāṉ kotaṇṭaṉ melmaṇi
nallūr tirivikkiramaco-

muṭaiyamātevarkku koṇṭu viṭṭa ku[ṭikkā]ṭu muṉpaṟṟāṉa nāṉ
koṇṭu uṭaiyakovaṉ coḻaṉuk[ku*] kuṭi-

niṅkātevatāṉamāka cammatittu kuṭimai kiḻiṟai iṟuttu uṇpāṉākavum
ippaṭikku kuṭiniṅkāteva-

tāṉam cantirātittavaṟ celvatāka ca[m*]matittu kuṭutteṉ veḷāṉ
kotaṇṭaneṉ .

inscription.head:
No. 368.
(A.R. No. 368 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL ROCK CUT TEMPLE—ON THE ROCK TO THE
NORTH OF THE CAVE (SHRINE)
RĀJ. TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA II—16th YEAR (A.D. 1161—62)
inscription.p:
This begins with introduction Pūmaruviya Tirumādum etc. The latter
portion of the inscription is damaged. It seems to record an endowment of
two mā of cultivable land (ūṟal-vayal) together with the surrounding barren and
saline soil after purchasing the same from the Śivabrāhmaṇas to the temple at
Tirunalakkuṉṟam in Kuṉṟiyūr-nāḍu by Munaiyilādittaṉ Kodaṇḍaṉ of Śīkaranallūr
in the same nāḍu. The donor appears to have already given a piece of land of
his own to the temple for the celebration of some festival in the temple and for
feeding Māhēśvaras in a maṭha founded (or built ?) by him.

l.ta:
pūmaruviya tirumātum puvimātum jayamātum nā maruviya
kalaimātum pukaḻmātum naya-

ntu pulka arumaṟaiyavar vitineṟiyaṉaittun taḻaittoṅka varumuṟai

vara villavariraṭṭar miṉavar ciṅkaḷar pallavar nutaliyar pāttivar paṇiya
veṇṇaruṅ kaṟpiṉ maṇṇakam purantu cempoṉvira-

inscription.head:
No. 369.
(A.R. No. 369 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL ROCK-CUT TEMPLE—ON THE ROCK TO THE
SOUTH OF THE SAME SHRINE.
inscription.p:
This is built in at the beginning. It is dated in the 9th year of a king
whose name is lost. It might probably be Uttama-Chōḷa. It seems to record
the gift of a quarter (vēli) of land free of taxes as dēvadāna to the temple of
Kūṟṟakkuḍi-Paramēśvara by the Ūrār of Peruṅkūṟṟakkuḍi on an order received
from Vīraśōḻa-Iḷaṅgō [vēḷār] (Koḍumbāḷūr chief) to provide for the daily
offerings to the deity. Mention is made in the body of the record, of
Madhurāntakadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 369A.
(A.R. No. 369-A of 1906)
inscription.p:
Intervening into the lines of the above inscription are the following four
lines in Pallava-Grantha characters (C. 7th century). The first two lines are
repetitions of the musical expressions kaiśika-madhyama a variety of svara and
Chatush-prahāra which is a rythmic form of four beats. This fragment is
evidently connected with No. 354 of 1904 which is engraved close by and which
is a treatise on music said to have been composed by one Rudrāchārya, whose
name is also given here in the third line.

inscription.head:
No. 370.
(A.R. No. 370 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL ROCK-CUT TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA
IN FRONT OF THE CAVE SHRINE.
TRIBH. RĀJARĀJADĒVA—5th YEAR
inscription.p:
This is much damaged. It records a gift of 96 sheep and 13 cows and
also a lampstand for burning a perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirumēṟṟali-
uḍaiya-Mahādēva at Tirunalakkuṉṟam by one Nūṟṟaṉ Periyāṉ alias Vīramaḻagiya-
Paraiyaṉ a (military) follower of Śīrāḷaṉ Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-Kaḍambarāyaṉ.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] tribhuvaṉaccakravattikaḷ śrī rājarājadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 5- vatu

l.ta: iraṭṭapāṭi koṇṭa coḻavaḷa nāṭṭu kunṟiyūr nāṭṭut tirunalakkunṟa-
mutali kaṭṭi nūṟṟa[n] periyānāna viram aḻakiyaparai(rai)yaneṉ vaitta ti-

runantāviḷakku onṟukku ko••••nāl ney uḻakkukku viṭakkaṭava
āṭu toṇṇāṟṟāṟu-
kku ikkoyil civappirāmaṇar pāri cū••• ṟanum nāṟpatteṇṇā-

[yira]k kūttan muṭivilāmutalum karumāṇi••••num cūṟṟi kunṟanum
a[y]ya[n]-
nūṟṟan periyānāna viramaḻakiya paraiya[ne]n itu pal

inscription.head:
No. 371.
(A.R. No. 371 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL ROCK-CUT TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
TRIBH. KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶADĒVA (I)—45th YEAR
(A.D. 1114-15).
inscription.p:
This records a gift of a lamp-stand and 96 sheep for burning a perpetual
lamp in the temple of Tirunalakkuṉṟam-Ūḍaiyār in Iraṭṭapāḍikoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu
by a Veḷḷāḷa resident of Kāraiyūr by name Veṭāṇ Poṉṉaṉ alias Sembiyan
Kōnāṭṭuvēḷāṉ and another gift for a similar purpose, of 4 mā of land at
Iluppaikkuḍi village made cultivable after its purchase from the temple itself by
Vēḷāṉ Poṟkāmaṉ alias Uttamaśōḻa. Mūvēndavēḷān of Iḍaiyāṟṟur in the same
nāḍu. Both the gifts are said to have been left in charge of the Sivabrāh-
manas of the temple.

sva sti śrī [||*] tiripuvaṉaccakravarttikaḷ śrī kolottuṅkacoḻadēvaṟku
yāṇṭu [4]5- āvatu iraṭṭapāṭikoṇṭa coḻavaḷanāṭṭu uṭaiyār tirunalak
kuṉṟamuṭaiyāṟku iṉṉāṭṭuk kār[ai]yūr veḷāṉ poṉṉaṉāṉa cempiyaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 372.
(A.R. No. 372 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL ROCK-CUT TEMPLE—ON THE SECOND GŌPUṚA.
RIGHT AT ENTRANCE.
TRIBH. VĪRAPĀṆḌYADĒVA—2nd YEAR
inscription.p:
This is an interesting document. It states that the temple treasury of
Tirunalakkuṉṟam Uḍaiya-nāyanār had been burgled by priests who stole away
the jewels and cash therefrom. The theft was found out and a meeting was
called of all the (representative) inhabitants of the nāḍu, nagaram and grāmam
of the region, who held an enquiry into the offair. one of the priests by
name Kuṉṟan Serundivanapperumāṉ confessed to have taken 60 poṉ and shared
it with a stone mason. He also implicated the others in the theft but they
denied all knowledge of the matter. They were thereupon taken to a court
at Mattukurichchi (?) presied over by a Sāmanta and ordered to prove their
innocence before the assembled people by holding a red-hot iron in their hands.
The ordeal went against them as their hands were burnt and they had to
confess their guilt. The culprits were all pronounced as Śiva drōhis and they
were deprived of their holdings at Iluppaik-kuḍi, which were now transferred
to the temple as Tirunāmattukkāṇi with the cultivating right thereon (kōyil-
uḻavu-muṟṟūṭṭu.) Such lands as had been sold by them to others were got
back on payment of their cost by the temple and those held on mortgage
were recovered without any compensation. Other private lands in the village
were to continue their payment of the achchuvari and other taxes into the
temple as usual.

inscription.head:
No. 373.
(A.R. No. 337 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL ROCK-CUT TEMPLE-IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
The writing of this inscription seems to be of about the 13th century A.D.
This is a verse in praise of the Pāṇḍya king (Teṇṇavaṇ) Śeyya-Perumāḷ
ruler of Madurai.

inscription.head:
No. 374.
(A.R. No. 374 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL ROCK-CUT TEMPLE-IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is another verse of the same period extolling the exploits of
Pāṇḍya (Mīnavarkōn) against the chōḷa king.

inscription.head:
No. 375.
(A.R. No. 375 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL ROCK-CUT TEMPLE ON THE SIDE OF
THE SECOND GŌPURA, LEFT OF ENTRANCE.
inscription.p:
This seems to record the acknowledgement (taravu-muṛi) of some
help rendered by a certain Kuḍumiyār Udaiyapperumāḷ of Śikaranallūr in
Teṉkōnāḍu to the Ūrār of Pulvayal and four other villages (Pulvayal Añjunilai)
in return for which he got the accountancy of the 5 villages.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 376.
(A.R. No. 376 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL ROCK-CUT TEMPLE-IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is a verse in praise of a Pāṇḍya chief (Kaṉṉi-vaḷanāḍaṉ) who defeated
This chōla adversary in a battle and forced him to flee from the field with his
legs seared (Karikālaṉ). The writing is of about the 13th Century A.D.

edition.l: kiyavar kāṇ |||—

inscription.head:
No. 377.
(A.R. No. 377 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL—ROCK-CUT TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is a verse by poet Ādināthar making fun of God Nalakkuṉṟar
with his matted hair (kuḍumi) enmeshing Gaṅgā.

inscription.head:
No. 378.
(A.R. No. 378 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL ROCK-CUT TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is dated in the cyclic year Vikrama which would correspond to
A.D. 1520-21 in the reign of Kṛishṇadēvarāya. The middle portion of the
inscription is covered by the doorway. It seems to record a gift of the
income from the village Iḍaituṟai to the temple of Tirunalakkuṉṟam-uḍaiya-
nāyaṉār for offerings and worship to the deity, made by (minister)
Tirumalaidēva Mahārāja at the request of Vālu-Nāyakkar, an officer of the
king's treasury.

inscription.head:
No. 379.
(A.R. No. 379 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL ROCK-CUT TEMPLE—ON THE ROCK
BEHIND THE VINĀYAKA SHRINE.
TRIBH. SUNDARA PĀṆḌYADĒVA—201st YEAR
inscription.p:
The king is evidently Māṟavarman Sundara I (acc. A.D. 1216) as in
No. 380 below. This registers a sale of 5 mā of land in the dēvadāṉa village
Tirumeyyam belonging to the temple of Tiruvikrama-Chōḷīsvaram-uḍaiya-
Nāyaṉār at Mēnmaṉainallūr in Kuṉṟiśūḻ-nāḍu by the trustees of the temple
and the Ūrār of the place, for 3200 kāśu, to Periyanāchchi Duggai, a dēvaraḍīyāḻ
of the temple at Tirunalakkuṉṟam. The proviso of the sale given at
the end is not clear.

edition.l: yateva[ṟ*]ku yāṇṭu 20- vatu etirāmāṇṭu

l.ta: ku nāṅkaḷ kalveṭṭik kuṭutta paṭiyāvatu iṉṉāyanār te-
vatāṉam tirumeyyattu••• yom perunāṉkellaikkuḷpaṭṭa nilam varu

inscription.head:
No. 380.
(A.R. No. 380 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
MĒLAIKKŌYIL-ROCK-CUT TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE.
MĀR. TRIBH. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYA I—21 + 1st YEAR
(A.D. 1236-37).
inscription.p:
This records a similar sale of another piece of land, 5 mā in extent,
in the same village, by the Ūrār and temple trustees to Umaiyāḻvi
Periyanāchchi. In this case they also agreed to pay the kīḻiṟai on the land
on behalf of the purchaser for an additional sum of money received by thēm.
It is possible that Periyanāchchi was the mother of Duggai mentioned in
No. 379 above.

inscription.head:
No. 381.
(A.R. No. 381 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE.
KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
AIYANĀR TEMPLE—ON A ROCK BEHIND
inscription.p:
This seems to be a verse commemorating the victory of the Pāṇḍya king
(Teṉṉavar kō) over a certain Vaḍugeṟinda Magadēśaṉ in battle, in which
however he lost his life (Viṭṭaindāṉ). The writing is of about the 13th century A.D.

edition.l: ntāṉat teṉṉarkoven•• ||—

inscription.head:
No. 382.
(A.R. No. 382 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLĪ DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
AIYANĀR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME ROCK.
inscription.p:
The last three lines of this inscription are much damaged. The writing
is of about the 13th Century A.D., This is a verse in praise of a chief by
name Vāṇakulōttamaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 383.
(A.R. No. 383 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
AIYANĀR TEMPLE-ON THE SAME ROCK
inscription.p:
This is a verse in praise of the chief Vīramāgadan Rājarājadēvan Pon-
parappināṉ Magadaipperumāḷ. It also mentions a fight between the chief Vāṇaṉ
Vaḍivēlāṉ and the Pāṇḍya king (Teṉṉavar-kōṉ)

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] viramākataṉ irā[ja*]rā[ja*]tevaṉ poṉparappiṉāṉ ma-

l.ta: peruveḷḷap peruñcāvi pokātākil iṭṭa tiṭṭukku o-
ṉavar koṉ vāṇaṉ vaṭivelān toṟṟuṭainta teṉṉa-

inscription.head:
No. 384.
(A.R. No. 384 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ON THE NORTH SIDE OF THE HILL IN THE VILLAGE,
NEAR A WELL.
inscription.p:
The inscription states that this spring (well) is the gift of Iṟaṅgalmīṭṭa
Tāḻaiyūr-Nāḍāḻvāṉ, son of Araiśuviṟṟirundāṉ Iḷaiyaraiyaṉ alias Iḍaiyāṟṟūr-
Nāḍāḻvān who had the kāval right over Tiruttavattuṟai in Iḍaiyāṟṟūr-nāḍu
a division of Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu. This place is evidently the same as the
present Lālguḍi.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] pāṇṭiyakulā-

inscription.head:
No. 385.
(A.R. No. 385 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, KUḌUMIYĀMALAI.
ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF THE HILL IN THE VILLAGE,
NEAR ANOTHER WELL
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged. It consists of a few verses eulogising the
exploits of the chiefs Vāṇarpirāṉ Rājarājaṉ, his son Ponparappina-perumāḷ;
and also of his descendants (?) Añjādaperumāḷ and a chief (name not clear)
who gets the epithet “Vaḍugeṟinda”

inscription.head:
No. 386.
(A.R. No. 386 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
ROCK-CUT ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH AND SOUTH WALLS.
inscription.p:
This consists of a number of fragmentary pieces of writing in Pallava-
Grantha script.
Published in South Indian Inscriptions Vol. XII, No. 7-A

inscription.head:
No. 387.
(A.R. No. 387 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
ROCK-CUT ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL.
MĀR. TRIBH. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA (II*)—7th YEAR (A.D. 1245).
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as [Ṛisha]bha, 13, śu. 10, Sunday, Uttiram
which would correspond to A.D. 1245, May 7.

The inscription gives details of the settlement of a long standing dispute
between the trustees of the two adjoining Śiva and Vishṇu temples of
Tirumeyyattu Mahādēva and Tirumeyyattu-Niṉṟaruḷiya-Perumāṉ, which had
resulted in the negleet of their lands and the stoppage of worship in both. A meeting
was held in the presence of Appaṇṇa-Daṇḍanāyaka, the brother-in-law of Ravidēva
Daṇḍanāyaka (who is said to have taken possession of the nāḍu on behalf of his lord
Hoysaḷa Vīra-Sōmēśvara), of all the representative inhabitants of the nāuḍ
nagaram and the Villages of Kāna-nāḍu alias Virudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu,
and several other important persons of whom a long list is given They sent for the
two parties concerned and asked for the accounts as they stood on that day. They
then arrived at an arrangement satisfactory to both. The paddy yield of some
lands common to them was to be shared in the ratio of two to three. Lands
at Ūṟṟiyūr and Maṅgalam except those specifically assigned to one or the other
were divided equally. Some dēvadāna lands belonging exclusively to the Vishṇu
temple were exchanged for those of another Śiva temple of the place called
Vānavaṉmādīśvaram-Uḍaiyār. Certain lands were to go to the priests of the
Vishṇu temple and certain others assigned to the Śiva temple. Lands at
Aṇḍakkuḍi and Perundurai were to continue in the name of Niṉṟaruḷiya-
Perumān as before. The spring to the east was to belong to this god and the
stone well near the bali-piṭha to Mahādēva. A dividing wall if erected between
the two temples, was to be built according to a specified plan, leaving the
western portion of the two temples open. Each temple was to bear its own
cost of maintaining the uvachchar playing music in the temples according to its
needs. Old stone inscriptions relating to each temple except those which had
been cancelled, were to be re-engraved in the respective temples having been
copied over from the other.

This arbitration of the dispute by Appaṇṇa-Daṇḍanāyaka and the mention
of the conquest of Kāna-nādu by Vīra-Sōmēśvara from an unknown adversary
in the reign and country of the Pāṇḍya king, deserve to be noted, as perhaps
showing the dependence of the latter upon the former and the friendly
relations of the two at the time.

edition.p:
There are four more lines mentioning names of signatories, but they are
hopelessly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 387A.
(A.R. No. 387 A of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
ROCK-CUT ŚIVA TEMPLE-IN THE SAME PLACE.
inscription.p:
This is built in at the beginning of lines and is also incomplete.
It is a verse inscription in characters of about the 13th century A.D. It
seems to be in praise of some merchant community whose members made
name and money in such cities as Vāruṇāpati, Vellūr Nellūr, Kāñchī,
Pūmpugār, Paḻaiyārai and Teṉ-Madurāpuri. It refers to a gift of lamp to
god Śiva at Madurai (Māṉēndiya-Karamuḍai-Marudar).

inscription.head:
No. 388.
(A.R. No. 388 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
ROCK-CUT TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
MĀR. TRIBH. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA (II)—10 + 1st YEAR
(A.D. 1249-50).
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged in places. It records an endowment of some
lands measuring 8 mā in extent, as ūrkkīḻ-īṟaiyili by the Sabhā of Tirumayyam
for the maintenance of the Uvachchar in the temple of Mahādēvar, at the
instance of the representatives of the Nāḍu, Nagaram and the villages. The
epigraph makes a reference to the settlement of the dispute mentioned in
No. 387 above. A certain Tiruvēṅgaḍattunambi is said to have made a represen-
tation to (daṇḍanāyakkar) Appaṇṇar on behalf of the temple on this matter,
in answer to which the above permission was accorded to the Sabhā for the
endowment.

inscription.head:
No. 389.
(A.R. No. 389 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
ROCK-CUT ŚIVA TEMPLE—AT THE ENTRANCE, RIGHT SIDE.
MĀR. TRIBH. SUNDARAPĀṆḌYADĒVA—8th YEAR.
inscription.p:
This is damaged, and the last few lines are built in. It records an
allotment of four pieces of land tax-free in favour of four priests of the
temple of Tirumeyyattu Mahādēva by the Sabhā of the place, for the conduct
of a festival on the day of Makhā in Māśi month. This is stated to be in
lieu of 30 kalam of paddy they were till now paying to the temple every
year evidently as interest on ten paḻaṅkāśu which had been originally
invested with them as endowment for that purpose, by a sāmanta (feudatory)
of the Chōḷa king Rājarāja (III?) by name Iruñjiṟai-uḍaiyāṉ

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tirupuvaṉa cakkaravatti-

nellu muppatiṉ kalamāka tirumeyyattu mahātevaṟkku tiru•••

inscription.head:
No. 390.
(A.R. No. 390 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM
ROCK-CUT ŚIVA TEMPLE—AT THE SAME ENTRANCE, LEFT SIDE.
RĀJĒNDRACHŌḶADĒVA—21ST YEAR
(A.D. 1032-33)
inscription.p:
The inscription is badly damaged. It records an endowment of 35 kāśu
to provide with the interest thereon, for the daily offerings to god Tirumeyyadēvar,
by Śālāpam Vidiviḍaṅgaṉ of Mānamaṅgalam a brahmadēya village in Viṟaikkūṟṟam,
a division of Śōḻamaṇḍalam. Mention is made of a coin called Rājēndraśōḻaṉ
māḍai. The epigraph begins with the usual introduction Tirumaṉṉi Vaḷara etc.

edition.l: svasti śrī [||*] tirumaṉṉi vaḷara iru•••••••••

l.ta: ṅkeḻil muṭiyum muṉṉavar pakkal teṉṉavar vaitta cuntaramuṭi
muṟaimaiyiṟ cūṭuṅ kulataṉamākiya palar pukaḻ muṭiyum ceṅka-

payaṅkoṭu paḻimika muyaṅkiyil mutukiṭ ṭoḷitta ceya ciṅkaṉaḷap peru-

inscription.head:
No. 391.
(A.R. No. 391 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
ROCK-CUT ŚIVA TEMPLE—IN THE SAME PLACE
RĀJARĀJA I—19th YEAR (A.D. 1003-04)
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged and built in and also incomplete. It begins
with the introduction Tirumagaḷpōla etc. It seems to record an endowment
of land made by the Sabhā of Tirumeyyam a dēvadāna-brahmadēya village
in Kāṉa-nāḍu said to be a division of Arumoḻidēva-vaḷanāḍu to provide for
worship and offerings to god Vishṇu-Bhaṭāraka consecreted in the temple of
Śrī Mūlasthānam-uḍaiya Mahādēva by a resident of the place by name Tiruvēṅgaḍa-
[Tātar].

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] tirumakaḷ po•••••••••

l.ta: m eṇṭicai nikaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamum tiṇṭiṟal ve•••••••••

taṉneḻil vaḷar ūḻiyu ḷellāyāṇṭun toḻutakaviḷaṅku miyāṇṭey
ceḻi-

inscription.head:
No. 392.
(A.R. No. 392 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
ROCK-CUT ŚIVA TEMPLE—ON THE ROCK TO THE WEST.
inscription.p:
This is a badly damaged epigraph with its beginning also lost. It is almost
a duplicate of No. 387 above with a good portion of what is given there lost
in this inscription. The text is however given there in part for comparison of
the two records.

inscription.head:
No. 393.
(A.R. No. 393 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
SATYAGIRINĀTHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA.
JAṬ. TRIBH. VĪRA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA (II)—4[5]th YEAR
inscription.p:
The details of the date are given as Dhanus, ba. 8, Wednesday, Hasta,
which would correspond to A.D. 1340, December 13. This is Jaṭ. Vīra-Pāṇḍya II
who ascended the throne in A.D. 1296.

This registers the sale of pāḍikāval right inclusive of the income from
some specified lands in the village, by the Sabhā of Tirumeyyam, in Kāṉa-nāḍu
alias Virudarāya-bhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu, to Mūvaṉ Kāḍappiḷḷai, a member of the
Valaiyar (community) of Mēlaikkurundaṇpiṟai of the Paḍaippaṟṟu (military
area ?) in the same nāḍu for a price of 200 paṇam called the Vaḻitiṟandār-
guḷigai. The specification of the income from the lands are not clear being lost
in the damaged portion of the record.

edition.l:
ḷ śrīvirapāṇṭiyatevarkku yāṇṭu 4[5]- vatu dhanu[ su nāya] ṟṟu
[a]parapakṣattu aṣṭamiyum

inscription.head:
No. 394.
(A.R. No. 394 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
SATYAGIRINĀTHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA.
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 1591, Saumya, Tai 5 (A.D. 1669). It records a
sale of land at Malukkaṉpākkam out of the tiruviḷaiyāṭṭam belonging to god
Tiruppadi Aḻagiyameyyar at Tirumeyyam, together with a house in the street
by the side of the Veṅgaikkuḷam tank, for 300 paṇam (called Tirugōkarṉam
Miṉṉal), to a certain Śōlaiyappa-Piḷḷai Gaṅgaiyāḍi of Vānavīraṉ of Madurai, who
seems to have been an agent appointed by Raghunātha-Narēndra for the
conduct of the early morning service in the temple instituted by the chief for
his own merit. A separate piece of land at Malukkaṉ-vayal is also mentioned
as having been assigned to one Vīrarāghava-Piḷḷai for management.

edition.l:
śubhamastu svastiśrī [||*] cakāttam 1591 itaṉmel cellāniṉṟa
cavumiya varuṣam tai mātam 5 tiyati tirumalai tiruppati nayiṉār tirumeyyamalaiyāḷar kovil
śrīpaṇṭārattuk kāriya[ttukaṭav]ārum••••amalattārum••• vārarumom vāna
viraṉ maturaiyil

inscription.head:
No. 395.
(A.R. No. 395 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM
SATYAGIRINĀTHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
JAṬ. TRIBH. PARĀKARAMA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA
5+7th YEAR
inscription.p:
The details of date given as [Kumbha], ba. 12, Sunday, Uttirāḍam corres-
pond to A.D. 1369, February 4, according to Kielhorn (Ep. Ind. Vol. IX, p. 225)
This recods the sale of the (tenancy) rights over some pieces of land by Śīrāḷa-
dēvar Munaiyadaraiyar Makkaṇayināṉ of Mēlūr in Kāna-nāḍu alias Virudarāja-
bhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu to one Tiruvuḍaiyar Piṟavikkunallār of Kuḷamaṅgalam in
Mēlai Iraṇiyamuṭṭa-nāḍu said to be a tiruvuḍaiyāṭṭam of Nāyaṉār Aḻagar, for
200 paṇam, which was the amount he had to pay to the latter as ‘marriage
money’ for marrying his sister Kāṇaviniyār. He is stated to have also sold his
rights over some other lands for 540 paṇam presumably for meeting the other
expense of his marriage like the presentation of aṅgabhūshaṇam to the bride and
similar items. These kāṇiyāṭchi lands comprised a tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam land of
Nāyaṉār Tirumeyya-Malaiyāḷan, one of Emberumāḷ, and a dēvadāna land of god
Piḷḷaiyār of a local shrine. The others belonged to private individuals.

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] ko[ccaṭai] paṉmarāṉa tripuvaṉacakravattikaḷ śrīparākkiṟama
pā[ṇṭiyate]varkku āṇṭu 5- vatiṉ

etir 7- vatu [kumbha] nāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu tuvātaciyum nāyaṟṟukkiḻamai
yum peṟṟa uttirāṭattu nāḷ

l.ta:
nāyaṉār aḻakar tiruvuṭaiyāṭṭamāṉa melai iraṇiyamuṭṭa nāṭṭu kuḷa
maṅkalamuṭaiyāṉ tiruvuṭaiyar

piṟavikku nallāṟku kāṉaṉāṭāṉa virutarācapayaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭu [me]lūr
cir[ā]ḷatevar muṉaiyataraiyar makkaṇāyi-

ṇam paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu ivar uṭap

paṇam irunūṟṟukkum kaiyyil

yāṭciyāvatu eṉ viḻukkāṭu nilattil-

inscription.head:
No. 396.
(A.R. No. 396 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
SATYAGIRINĀTHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
JAṬ. TRIBH. PARĀKRAMA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA
5+7th YEAR
inscription.p:
This registers the alienation in favour of his son Śīrāḷadēvaṉ of the
kāṇiyāṭchi rights with all their appurtenances on a certain specified tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam
(and other ?) lands at Mēlśārkuḷam, Dēvimaṅgalam and Mēlūr, and two houses
in the latter two places, together with a number of slaves, by Śīrāḷadēvan
Muṉaiyadaraiyaṉ Makkaṇāyaṉ of Mēlūr in Kāṉa-nāḍu. As this person who
now makes a gift to his son, of some of his possessions, has figured in No.
395 above, which is of the same date as the present inscription, it is to be
understood that his contemplated marriage referred to in that record was a
later and second one.

inscription.head:
No. 397.
(A.R. No. 397 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
SATYAGIRINATHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is dated merely in the cyclic year Raudri without giving its Śaka
equivalent. It records a decision of Daḷavāy Vaiyappa-Nāyakkar, the agent of
Timmappa-Nāyakkar, and the managers of the temple of Tirumeyyam Malaiyāḷaṉ,
that the income from Iruñjiṟaiyūr which was a tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam village of the
god was to be utilised (in future solely) for the repairs of the temple.

edition.l: śubhamastu [||*] ro[kti]-

inscription.head:
No. 398.
(A.R. No. 398 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
SATYAGIRINĀTHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged. It records the endowment of the village
Koṭṭaiyūr renamed Raghunāthapuram for the conduct of the early morning
service in the temple of Aḻagiyameyyar at Tirumeyyam by Śōlaiyappa-Piḷḷai
[Gaṅ*]gaivāḍiyā-Piḷḷai of Vānaravīraṉ-Madurai, for the merit of Tirumalai
Sētupatikātta Daḷavāy Raghunātha-Narēndra. This is dated in the same year as
No. 394 above, and is additional to the gift mentioned therein. The donor
is said to have purchased the lands at Kōṭṭaiyūr from the mahājanas of the
place and having reclaimed them from their wild and fallow condition, settled
several Veḷḷāḷas in the place for their cultivation, before making this grant to
the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 399.
(A.R. No. 399 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM
SATYAGIRINĀTHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA
inscription.p:
Kṛishṇadēva-Mahārāya-Śaka 1444. The details of the date are given as
Chitrabhānu, Uttarāyaṇa, Makara-Saṅkramaṇa. (=AD. 1522, December 28).

The inscription registers a sarvamānya grant made to the two temples
of Aḻagiyameyyar (Vishṇu) and Tirumeyyadēvar (Śiva) at Tirumeyyam in Kāṉa-
nāḍu, by Chellappa Vīra Narasiṅgarāya-Nāyakkar (The kings viceroy in the
south), of all the lands in the village which had been lying as Paṇḍāravāḍai
(except those which had been already endowed as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam or dēvadāna).
As was the case with regard to all lands possessed by these two temples in
common, the income derived from the present lands was also to be shared
by them in the proportion of three and two respectively.

edition.l:
śubhamastu svasti [||*] śrīmanmahāmaṇḍalēśvaran harihara rāyara vibhāṭan
bhāṣaikkut tappuvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ muvarāyar kaṇṭaṉ kaṇṭanāṭu koṇṭu koṇṭa
nāṭu koṭātāṉ pūrvadakṣiṇa vaścimauttarasatussamudrādhipati śrīkraṣṇadēvamahārāyar
prathivirājyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa śakābdam 1444- ṉmel cellāniṉṟa citrabhānu
