inscription.head:
No. 400.
(A.R. No. 400 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
SATYAGIRINĀTHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE EAST WALL OF THE
SĒNAIMUDALIYĀR SHRINE
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 1383, Vishu, Uttarāyaṇa, Kumbha, śu. 6, Friday,
Aśvati corresponding to A.D. 1462, February 5. This registers an endowment
of the village Rāhuttamiṇḍaṉ-vayal alias Sundara-Pāṇḍyanallūr near Iḷañjar
village with all its lands wet and dry, and other appurtenances, for the requirements
of worship and offerings to god Tirumeyya-Malaiyāḷar, at Tirumeyyam in
Kānanāḍu alias Vīrudarājabhayaṅkara-vaḷanāḍu, during a service instituted in
his name in the temple, by the chief Avaiyāṇḍār alias Sundara-Pāṇḍya-
Vijayālayadēvar of Suraikkuḍi near Niyamam in Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍu, a division
of Kēraḷaśiṅga-vaḷanāḍu.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||]śakāptam 1383- ṉ mel cellāniṉṟa

l.ta: ti nāḷ keraḷa ciṅkavaḷanāṭṭu ataḷaiyūr nā-
ṭṭu niyamappaṟṟu curaikkuṭi avaiyāṇṭārāṉa cuntarapāṇṭiya vicaiyā-
laiyatevareṉ kāṉaṉāṭāṉa virutarāca payaṅkara vaḷanāṭṭu kāṉaṉā-
laiyāḷaṟku nammuṭaiya nāḷile nammuṭaiya perāle cuntarapāṇ-
ṭiya vicaiyālaiyaṉ cantiyāka iṉṉamutu veḷḷam eḻuntaruḷiṉā-
ppole amutu ceytaruḷum taḷikai oṉṟukku amutupaṭi neyyamu-
tu kaṟi amutu uppamutu miḷakamutu ilai amutu aṭaikkāyamutu cāttuppaṭi
tirumālai tirupparivaṭṭam uḷḷiṭṭa pala veñcaṉātikaḷukkum iṉṉāṭṭup
pakaippaṟṟu iḷañcārp puravil irākuttamiṇṭaṉ vayalāṉa cuntara-
ceyum nattamum nattatapparappuṅ kuḷamuṅ kuḷapparappu maṟṟum eppe-
māka cantiṟātittavar cella caṟu[va] māṉiyamāka naṭakkumpaṭi kaṟpit-
tu viṭṭa aḷavukku immariyātiyile iḷañcār puravil irāku-
riṟaiyili niṅkalākavum inta nilaṅkaḷil paccaiyum iruñciṟai-
peruntuṟaikku aṟṟa mariyāti iṉṉāyiṉāṟku viṭṭa aḷavukku-

l.note: This last line is wanting in the inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 401.
(A.R. No. 401 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
SATYAGIRINĀTHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL.
inscription.p:
This is merely dated in the cyclic year Jaya, Āṉi 20. It records the
conferment of the kāṇiyāṭchi right over a tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam land called Gōvindavayal
by the temple authorities of Nayinār Tirumeyya[ḻagar] on two persons by name
Tirumēni Vīrapāṇḍi-Āśāri and Meyya Maṇavāḷa-Āśāri for certain services to be
rendered by them in the temple.

edition.l: śubhamastu [||*] ceya varuṣam āṉi

l.ta: ṟam nilam taṭimuṟṟum ivaṉukku kāṇi ā-
iraṇṭu tavaḷaic caṅkamum oru tāriyum ti-

inscription.head:
No. 402.
(A.R. No. 402 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
SATYAGIRINĀTHA-PERUMAḶ TEMPLE—ON A STONE SET UP
INSIDE THE PREMISES
inscription.p:
This is in characters of about the 9th century A.D. It is incomplete.
It seems to record a gift of land for the renovation of some structure (temple ?)
and worship therein by Perumpiḍugu Perundēvi, the mother of (a chief by name)
Viḍēlviḍugu Viḻuppēradi-Araiśaṉ alias Śāttaṉ Māṟaṉ. It is possible that this
chief was related to the Muttaraiyars of Śendalai.

inscription.head:
No. 403.
(A.R. No. 403 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, TIRUMAYYAM.
SATYAGIRINĀTHA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SAME STONE
RĀJARĀJA I.
inscription.p:
This is a badly damaged fragment containing only a portion of the
introduction Tirumagaḷpōla etc. of Chōḷa Rājarāja I.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tirumakaḷ polap peruni[lac celviyum taṉakke urimai
pūṇ]ṭamai maṉa[kkoḷak]kāntaḷūrccālaiy

inscription.head:
No. 404.
(A.R. No. 404 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, PERUNDUṞAI
(near TIRUMAYYAM)
RUINED SIVA TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL
JAṬ. SUNDARA-CHŌḶA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA
12th YEAR

edition.p: Published in the Inscriptions of Pudukkottai State, No. 242.

inscription.head:
No. 405.
(A.R. No. 405 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, PERUNDUṞAI
(near TIRUMAYYAM)
SATYANĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL
TRIBH. KULASĒKHARADĒVA—13+13th YEAR
inscription.p:
Stones containing this inscription are misplaced and some are lost. This
seems to register an endowment of land (at Veḷḷūr) placed in-charge of the
Śrīvaishṇavas of the temple of Kaṇḍidēva-Viṇṇagar at Perunduṟai in Kāṉa-nāḍu,
by Kaṇḍaṉ Aḻagukaṇḍa-Perumāḷ and his elder brother Kaṇḍaṉ Āḷuḍaiyāṉ alias
Kalvāyil-Nāḍāḻvāṉ to provide for the worship of god Tavamuḍaiya-Pāṇḍīśar
consecrated by them (it is not clear where).

inscription.head:
No. 406.
(A.R. No. 406 of 1906)
TIRUCHIRAPALLI DISTRICT, PUDUKKOTTAI STATE, PERUNDUṞAI
(near TIRUMAYYAM)
SATYANĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBH. KULASĒKHARADĒVA—13+9th YEAR
inscription.p:
Uninscribed stone is built into the body of this inscription and its
continuation is lost. It seems to record a gift of some pieces of land made
tax-free to the temple of Tyāgamīkāma-Viṇṇagar-Emberumāṉ at Perunduṟai by
Kaṇḍaṉ Āḷuḍaiyāṉ Kalvāyil-Nāḍālvāṉ, the chief who figures in No. 405 above.

tirumeṟkoyil tiyākamikāma viṇṇakar remperumāṉukku [ciri] kaṇṭaṉ āḷu-

inscription.head:
No. 407.
(A.R. No. 407 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, MARUGĀLTALAI.
ON A BOULDER IN THE MIDDLE OF HILL KNOWN AS
PŪVILUḌAIYĀR-MALAI
inscription.p:
This is an inscription of one line in early Brāhmī characters (of the Aśokan
period) engraved on the overhanging rock of a cave on the floor of which are
cut seven stone beds, popularly known as Pāṇḍavar-paḍukkai. This along with
similar inscriptions from other places in the South have been published by
Shri K.V. Subrahmanya Aiyar in a paper read before the Oriental Conference
held at Madras in 1924.

inscription.head:
No. 408.
(A.R. No. 408 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚIYALAPPERI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
JAṬ. CHŌḶA-PĀṆḌYA—3rd YEAR
inscription.p:
Published in South Indian Inscriptions.
Vol. XIV No. 195.

inscription.head:
No. 409.
(A.R. No. 409 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚIVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
ŚAḌAIYA MĀṞAṈ—2 + 19th YEAR
inscription.p:
Published in South Indian Inscriptions.
Vol. XIV No. 72

inscription.head:
No. 410.
(A.R. No. 410 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚIVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
JAT. SUNDARA-CHŌḶA-PĀṆḌYA—Date lost
inscription.p:
Published in South Indian Inscriptions.
Vol. XIV No. 178

inscription.head:
No. 411.
(A.R. No. 411 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚIVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
RĀJARĀJA I—28th YEAR (A.D. 1012-13)
inscription.p:
This is in Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters. It is damaged. It begins with the usual
introduction Tirumagaḷpōla, etc. This records a tax-free gift of land at Kūḍalūr
in Murappu-nāḍu to the temple of Teṉ-Tirumāliruñjōlai Emberumāṉ on the
north bank of the river Taṇporunam (Tāmraparṇi), after purchasing the same,
by Kaiyānāyakaṉ Nāraṇamasūrā (?) of Nechchura-nāḍu, a division of Rājarāja-
maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 412.
(A.R. No. 412 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚĪVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
RĀJARĀJA I—28th YEAR (A.D. 1012-13)
inscription.p:
This is a Vaṭṭeḻuttu inscription. It is mutilated. It seems to record a
gift of land tax-free to the temple of Teṉ-Tirumāliruñjōlai-Emberumāṉ by three
individuals of the place.

edition.p: The next two lines are badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 413.
(A.R. No. 413 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚĪVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
RĀJARĀJA I—28th YEAR (A.D. 1012-13)
inscription.p:
This is also in Vaṭṭeḻuttu script and is mutilated. It seems to record an
endowment of land by the nāṭṭār of Murappu-nāḍu in Rājarāja-maṇḍalam to the
temple of Emberumāṉ at Teṉ-Tirumāliruñjōlai on the north bank of the
[Taṇporu]ndam river to provide for the expenses of a big feast in the temple on
the day following the day of asterism Uttirāḍam in Āḍi month and for burning
lamps.

••••••••• c celviyum taṉakkey urimai[pūṇṭamai maṉak]koḷa
kā[n]taḷūrcālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭuṅ kaṅkapāṭi

mum eṇṭicai pukaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamu••

inscription.head:
No. 414.
(A.R. No. 414 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVEḺI TALUK, ŚIVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE
RĀJARĀJA I—23rd YEAR (A.D. 1006-03)
inscription.p:
This is also in Vaṭṭeḻuttu. It is mutilated and stops with the mention
of god Karumāṇikkadēvar “who was pleased to have his abode” in Teṉ-
Tirumāliruñjōlai (a quarter ?) of Tirumukkūḍal in Kīḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam, a division
of Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
••••••••• ṉakkoḷa kāntaḷūrccālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭum
kaṅkapāṭiyu nuḷam[pa*]pāṭiyum taṭikaipāṭiyum kuṭamalaināṭum kollamuṅ kaliṅkamu-

••••••••• mum tiṇṭiṟal veṉṟi taṇṭāṟkoṇṭu taṉṉeḻil
vaḷarūḻiyuḷ ellāyāṇṭum toḻutakai viḷaṅkum yāṇṭey ceḻiyarait

••••••••• ṇṭu irupattumuṉṟāvatu irājarājavaḷanāṭṭu kiḻkaḷa
kūṟṟattu tirumukkūṭalt teṉ tirumāliruñcolai niṉṟaruḷiṉa karumāṇikka tevark

inscription.head:
No. 415.
(A.R. No. 415 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚIVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
RĀJ. [VĪRARĀJĒNDRADĒVA]—Date lost.
inscription.p:
This is a fragment containing a portion of the introduction of Vīra-
rājēndra-Chōḷa beginning with the expression Viramē tuṇaiyāgavum, and of the
inscription proper, recording a gift of 50 sheep for burning a perpetual lamp in
the temple of Ten-Tirumāliruñjōlai-Dēvar in Kīḻkaḷakkūṟṟam.

l.ta:
tu kūṭal caṅkattākavamallaṉai añcuvittu—vikkalaṉai
yum caṅkalaṉaiyum muṟai-

—ṉ niṟai āḻākku ney aṭṭakkaṭavāṉ caṭaiyaṉ cittaṉ ivvuḻakku
ney-

inscription.head:
No. 416.
(A.R. No. 416 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚIVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
CHAKRAVARTI KULŌTTUṄGA-CHŌḶA (II ?)—35th YEAR
inscription.p:
This records a gift of sheep (or cows) by Nāraṇaṉ Poliñjāṉ, a resident of
Jayaṅgoṇḍa-śōḻapuram in Valla-nāḍu, for the merit of his wife, for burning a
lamp in the temple of Karumāṇikka-Āḻvār at Teṉ Tirumāliruñjōlai in Kīḻkaḷak-
kūṟṟam which is stated to be a sub-division of Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu, which
formed again a division of Rājarāja-Pāṇḍināḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] cakkaravattikaḷ śrī kolāttuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu
35- vatu rājarājappāṇ-

colai niṉṟaruḷiṉa

vinumaimān poliñca••••yai cā[t*]ti•• viḷakkonṟukkum
ne[y*]yāḷākku ca

inscription.head:
No. 417.
(A.R. No. 417 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚĪVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
RĀJARĀJA I—Date lost
inscription.p:
The inscription is badly mutilated and damaged. It seems to record some
provision made by the Sabhā of the place for (special) offerings and worship to
god Tirumāliruñjōlai-Dēvar on the day of Uttirāḍam in the Āḍi month.

l.ta:
••••••••• pāṭiyum taṭikaipāṭiyum kuṭamalai nāṭum kollamuṅ
kaliṅka•••••••••

yāṇṭey ceḻiyarai tecukoḷ ko irāja••••

inscription.head:
No. 418.
(A.R. No. 418 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚĪVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
RĀJARĀJA I—29th YEAR (A.D. 1013-14)
inscription.p:
This begins with introduction Tirumagaḷpōla etc. It records a gift of a
lampstand and 25 sheep for burning a lamp in the temple of Karumāṇikkadēvar
by the wife of a merchant by name Rāmaṉ Lakkuvaṇaṉ of Karavandapuram in
Kaḷakkuḍi-nāḍu. Teṉ Tirumāliruñjōlai is said to be in Kīḻkaḷakkūṟṟam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tirumakaḷpola perunilaicelviyum taṉakke urimai [pūṇ]
ṭamai [maṉakkoḷa] kāntaḷurcālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭum kaṅkapāṭiyum

inscription.head:
No. 419.
(A.R. No. 419 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚĪVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
RĀJARĀJA I—24th YEAR (1008-09 A.D.)
inscription.p:
The inscribed stones are built out of order. The epigraph begins with
introduction Tirumagaḷpōla etc. It records a gift (of money ?) by Araiyaṉ
Kumaraṉ of Paṉaṅguḍi in Paṭṭiṉakkūṟṟam a division of Kshatriyaśikhāmaṇi-
vaḷanāḍu on the southern side of the Kāvēri river in Śōḻanāḍu, for burning a
perpetual lamp in the temple of Tirumāliruñjōlai-Dēvar. The writing is Vaṭṭeḻuttu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tirumakaḷpola perunilaccelviyun taṉakke urimai pūṇṭamai
maṉakkoḷak kāntaḷūrccālai kalamaṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭuṅ kaṅkapāṭiyu nuḷampapāṭiyum
taṭikaipāṭiyu-

m kuṭamalaināṭum kollamu[m*] kaliṅkamu[m*] eṇṭicai pukaḻtara īḻa

inscription.head:
No. 420.
(A.R. No. 420 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚIVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
RĀJARĀJA I—23rd YEAR (A.D. 1007-08)
inscription.p:
This begins with Tirumagaḷpōla introduction. This is a Vaṭṭeḻuttu inscrip-
tion which is mutilated towards the end. It seems to record a gift (of 15 cows ?)
for a perpetual lamp in the temple at Tirumāliruñjōlai in Kīḻkaḷakkūṟṟam said
to be a division of Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, by a native of Tirunelvēli in Kīḻvembai-
nāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tirumakaḷpolap perunilaccelviyun taṉakke urimai
[pūṇṭa]mai maṉakkoḷak kāntaḷūrc [cā]lai kalama[ṟu]ttaruḷi veṅkaināṭuṅ kaṅka
pāṭi[yu nu*]

īḻamaṇṭalamum iraṭṭapāṭi eḻarai ilakkamun tiṇṭiṟal ve[ṉ]•••••••••

•••••••••

inscription.head:
No. 420A.
(A.R. No. 420-A of 1906)
inscription.p:
This is a fragment of two lines of writing in Tamil of about the 12th
century A.D., and is dated in the 23rd year of some king whose name is lost.
It mentions two vēli of land by name Śōḻapāṇḍya-viḷāgam which had been given
as tax-free dēvadāna to the temple of Karumāṇikkattāḻvār at Uttamaśōḻa-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam said to be a brahmadēya in Kīḻkaḷakkūṟṟam a division of
Muḍigoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Rājarājappāṇḍināḍu. This is found engraved just
above No. 420.

l.ta:

nāṭṭuk kiḻkkaḷakkūṟṟattu

—y niṉṟaruḷiṉa karumāṇikkattāḻvā[r*]kku coḻapāṇṭiyaviḷākam
perāl iṟaiyili tevatāṉamāka variyiliṭṭu va

nilam iruveliyum muṉpu nāṅkaḷ
kāṭṭiykuṭutta nilam

inscription.head:
No. 421.
(A.R. No. 421 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚIVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
ŚAḌAIYA-MĀṞAṈ—2 + 19th YEAR
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XIV No. 71

inscription.head:
No. 422.
(A.R. No. 422 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, ŚIVALAPPĒRI.
AḺAGAR TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH AND WEST WALLS OF THE
CENTRAL SHRINE
RĀJARĀJA I—26th YEAR (A.D. 1016-11)
inscription.p:
Pieces are missing in the middle of the inscription. This begins with the
usual introduction of the king (Tirumagaḷpōla etc.). It records the endowment
of a land called Kuḷakkuḍikkuḷam on the north bank of the river Taṇporundam,
free of all taxes, to provide for the expenses of the tiruchcheṉṉaḍai (daily
procession with lights inside the prākāra of the temple) of god Karumāṇikka-
dēvar “who was pleased to reside” in Teṉ-Tirumāliruñjōlai, by the Mahāsabhā
of Śrīvallabhamaṅgalam (Śīvalappēri) a brahmadēya in Kiḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam a division
of Rājarāja-maṇḍalam.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tirumakaḷpolap perunilaiccelviyun taṉakke urimai
pūṇṭamai maṉakkoḷak kāntaḷūrccālai ka[la*]maṟuttaruḷi veṅkaiñāṭuṅ

l.ta:
ṭikaipāṭiyuṅ kuṭamalaināṭuṅ kollamum
kaliṅkamum eṇṭicai pukaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamum iraṭṭapāṭi

ṇṭiṟalveṉṟit taṇṭāṟ koṇṭu taṉṉeḻil vaḷar
ū[ḻi]uḷ ellāyāṇṭu

maiyilt tirucceṉṉaṭaikkum tiru
viḷakkukkumāka niroṭu aṭṭikkuṭutta pulakkuḷa—ṟṟukkiṭan

ttiṉ vaṭaka[rai]k kuḷakkuṭikkuḷam vaḷaiyilc cuṟṟumuṟṟum māḷni—••••m
meṉokkiṉa maramuṅ kiḻṇokki[ya] kiṇaṟum puṟṟuñ ce-

kuḷattukkuppe—lai parāṅkucamaṅkalattu ellaikkuk kiḻakkum vaṭavellai ammāṉ[ku]-

naṭuvukiṭan

kuḷattiṉ ki-

vittar paṇikka ātaṉūr nārāyaṇa[ṅ] kecuvapaṭṭar paṇikka paṇiyiṉālp paṇiccum
dhanmadāṉa olai— mahāsabhaiyom mahāsabhaiyār paṇikka ivvolai eḻutiṉe-

taiṉūṟṟuva maṅkalātittaṉ eḻuttu ||| .

inscription.head:
No. 423.
(A.R. No. 423 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, MĀṈŪR.
AMBALAVĀṆASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON ONE OF THE PILLARS
INSIDE THE TEMPLE
MĀṞAÑJAḌAIYAṈ—35th YEAR, 469th day.
inscription.p: Published in South Indian Inscriptions, Vol. XIV, No. 37.

inscription.head:
No. 424.
(A.R. No. 424 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, MĀṈŪR.
AMBALAVĀṆASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON A SECOND PILLAR
INSIDE THE TEMPLE
TRIBH. KŌNĒRINMAIKOṆḌĀṈ—2 + 1st YEAR
inscription.p:
This registers an order of the king making a gift of about two vēli of land
at Mānanilainallūr in Kaḷakkuḍi-nāḍu to the temple of Kulaśēkhara-Viṇṇagar-
Āḻvār at Karuvānallūr alias Vikrama Pāṇḍyanallūr in the same nāḍu, at the
request of (minister) Kāliṅgarāyar, to meet the expenses of worship and offerings
connected with the service called the Maṉamperiyāṉ-sandi instituted in the latter's
name in the temple. Kāliṅgarāyar is referred to as a native of Poṉpaṟṟi near
Pullūrkuḍi in Naḍuviṟkūṟu a division of Miḻalaik-kūṟṟam. The land is said to
have been hitherto a veṭṭiyāṉ jīvitam but now converted into an iṟaiyili-dēvadāna
with the kārāṇmai (tenancy) right granted to the Sabhā of Māṉanilainallūr, the
kaḍamai and all other dues going to the temple. The record seems to be
assignable to king Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara I.

edition.l: eḻuttu ||—

inscription.head:
No. 425.
(A.R. No. 425 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, MĀṈŪR.
AMBALAVĀṆASVĀMI TEMPLE—AT THE TOP OF AN IMAGE
SET UP IN FRONT
inscription.p:
This is a label in Vaṭṭeḻuttu of two lines, with the figure of a man with
outstretched arms of which the left holds a sword.

inscription.head:
No. 426.
(A.R. No. 426 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, MĀṈŪR.
AMBALAVĀṆASVĀMI TEMPLE—AT THE TOP OF A SECOND IMAGE
IN FRONT
inscription.p:
This is also a label inscription of three lines of writing. The figure is
that of a man holding a club in each of his hands. The inscription refers to a
threatened destruction of the village and its rescue by one Vaḷaiñjiyaṉ Iḷaiyāṉ
Kāḍaṉ who fell in the attempt.

inscription.head:
No. 427.
(A.R. No. 427 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, MĀṈŪR.
AMBALAVĀṆASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON A PILLAR
SET UP IN FRONT
inscription.p:
The inscription is built in at its lower end by the platform on which it
stands. It mentions the trading community called the Diśai Āyirattaññūṟṟuvar
who claim themselves to be the children (devotees) of goddess Paramēśvari of
Aiyapoḻil-pura and whose members had business interest in a number of places.
A list of them is given below. 1. Tirukkōḷūr alias Lōkachintāmaṇipuram in
Tiruvaḻudivaḷanāḍu 2. Kaṇṇaṉūr alias Karikālaśoḻapuram 3. Paṭṭaṇamarudūr
alias Kēraḷāntakapuram 4. Mānavīrapaṭṭaṇam alias Dēśi Uttamachōḷapaṭṭaṇam
5. Vīrapāṇḍyapuram alias Dēśi Ayyapaṭṭaṇam 6. Veḷḷūr alias Rājarājapuram
7. Pūvēndiyaśōḻapuram in Aḻagiyaśēkhara-vaḷanāḍu 8. Śivacharaṇaśēkhara-perun-
deruvu in Kīḻvēmbu-nāḍu 9. Dēśi Uyyavandapaṭṭaṇam and 10. Rājavallapuram.

The purpose of the record is however not known, being lost in the built
in portion.

inscription.head:
No. 428.
(A.R. No. 428 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, MĀṈŪR.
AMBALAVĀṆASVĀMI TEMPLE—ON ANOTHER FACE OF THE
SAME PILLAR
inscription.p:
This is unfinished and damaged. The contents are similar to those of
No. 427 above.

inscription.head:
No. 429.
(A.R. No. 429 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, MĀṈŪR.
MĀḌAI-NĀCHCHIYĀR SHRINE NEAR THE TANK—ON A STONE
LYING IN FRONT
MĀR. SUNDARA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA—8 + 1st YEAR
inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged. It seems to record a tax-free gift of land
made by the Sabhā of the place (purpose not clear) at the request of Kōrai
Śēndaṉ alias Kēraḷādityadēva of Kuḍamaṅgalam in Malaimaṇḍalam who is stated
to be a local officer at Māṉanilainallūr in Kaḷakkuḍi-nāḍu, for the welfare of
Buddattiruvaḍi of Tiruppāppūr.

edition.l: [svasti] śrī [||*] komāṟapaṉmarāṉa
8- vatu etirāmāṇṭu taimm[ātam]

inscription.head:
No. 430.
(A.R. No. 430 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, MĀṈŪR.
ON A HERO-STONE SET UP NEAR THE
BUND OF THE TANK
inscription.p:
This is badly damaged. The writing seems to be of about the 13th cen-
tury A.D. and the inscription is dated in the 41st year of an unspecified king.
This seems to record the grant, as Udirappaṭṭi of one mā each of land to
(the survivors of) three persons by name Kuḍavaṉ Śāttaṉ Appaṉ, Niṉṟamāl
Kāvidi and Pāli Vēḷāṉ (who lost their lives evidently in trying to save the village
from the devastation of flood in the great river), by the local chieftain
(Nāḍāḻgiṉṟa) Gaṅgaikoṇḍaśōḻa-Pe[rumā]kkaḷ (?). The Mahāsabhā of Marutān-
taka (Madhurāntaka)-chaturvēdimaṅgalam is stated to have remitted the taxes
due on the lands granted.

edition.l: maṅkala[ ttu ma] hāsabhai-
tta nappanum yāṇṭu 41- āva-

inscription.head:
No. 431.
(A.R. No. 431 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, SRIVAIKUNTHAM TALUK, MURAPPANĀḌU.
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆA PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH WALL
OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
TRIBH. KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ—11th + 1st YEAR
inscription.p:
This is an incomplete inscription which might be ascribed to Māṟavarmaṉ
Sundara-Pāṇḍya II. It states that at the request of minister Maḻavarāyaṉ, the
king granted the taxes due to him on (3/4) vēli and one mā and half of land at
Māṇikuḍi, a hamlet of Śrīvallabhamaṅgalam in Kīḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam, which was in
the enjoyment of a certain Āli Villavadaraiyaṉ of Vaḻugūr, to the temple of
Sōmidēva-viṇṇagar-āḻvār at Sōmidēva-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in
Murappu-nāḍu for the requirements of worship to the deity. The consolidated
dues payable on the land both for the kār and paśānam crops are given as
[2] kalam, 1 tūṇi and 1 padakku of paddy on every mā of land (wet) and
2 diramam on every mā of (dry) land growing sesamum, millet, etc.

l.ta:
vi[ṇṇa]karāḻvār kOyil
nampuceyvāṉukkum śrīvaiṣṇavarkaḷukkum ivvāḻvāṟku tiruppaṭimāṟṟuḷḷiṭṭu
veṇṭum nivantaṅkaḷukku vaḻukū-

maṅkalattu
mā[ṇi]kuṭiyil kāṇiyāṉa nilam mukkāle yorumāvaraiyum viṉiyokamun taruvatāṉa
accum kāriyavārācciyum veṭṭipāṭṭamum pañcupili cantivikkirakappeṟum poṉvariyum
maṟṟum meppe-

yarpaṭṭaṉavumuṭpaṭa patiṉoṉṟā[vati]ṉetirāmāṇṭu mutal tevatāṉa
iṟaiyili[yāy] iṟuppatāka iṭappeṟa veṇumeṉṟum i[n]nilam iṟukku miṭattu
nilam mukkāle yoru māvum nilamaḷantu payirpāṟttu viḷainta nilattukku kaṭamaiyum
viṉiyokamun taruvatāṉa accum kāri-

yavārācciyum veṭṭipāṭṭamu[m] pañcupili cantivikkirakappeṟum poṉ[vari]
yu[m*] maṟṟu meppeyarpaṭṭaṉavu muṭpaṭa ivvūr tarappaṭi kārukku māttāl nellu
••kalaṉe tūṇippatakkākavum••• eḷ varaku tiṉai viḷainta nilattukku māttāl
tiramam iraṇṭā-

kavum pacāṉamum ivvaricai [yi]ṟuppatākavum iṟai kaṭṭaveṇumeṉṟu[m
ai]yyaṉ maḻavarāyar namakkuc coṉṉamaiyil ivvāḻvāṟku tiruppaṭimāṟṟuḷḷiṭṭu
veṇṭum nivantaṅkaḷukku vaḻukūruṭaiyāṉ āli villavataraiyaṉukku kiḻkaḷa-

edition.l:
kkūṟṟattu śrīvallabhama[ṅkalat]tu maṇaṟkuṭiyil kāṇiyāṉa nilam mu[kkā]le
orumāvum kaṭamaiyum viṉiyokamun taruvatāṉa accum kāriyavārācciyum [veṭṭi
pāṭṭa]mum pañcupili cantivikkirakappeṟum poṉvariyum maṟṟu meppeyarpa
(īncomplete)

inscription.head:
No. 432.
(A.R. No. 432 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, SRIVAIKUNTHAM TALUK, MURAPPANĀḌU.
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
TRIBH. KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ
inscription.p:
The inscription is mutilated. It seems to record an order of the king
to the Sabhā of Sōmidēva-chaturvēdimaṅgalam authorising them to collect every
year from the temple of Tirunelvēli-uḍaiyār, 140 kalam of paddy evidently due
to the royal treasury as taxes, to make up for the deficiency in the amount
required for the celebration of a ten-day festival in the temple of Vēdanāyakar.
Mention is made of a certain Yādavasiṁha-bhaṭṭaṉ of Prāndūr who had the right
of worship in the temple, and to whom this paddy was to be made over towards
the expenses of the festival. Reference is also made to a donor by name
Araṅganambi, a Malayāḷa brāhmaṇa but the nature of his gift is not clear.
Paḻandīparāyaṉ and Pallavarāyaṉ are two of the signatories at the end.

l.ta: ti uṭaiyaṉāy aṉupavittuvarukiṟa paṅku oṉṟum malaiyā[ḻa]

ṇaṉ
araṅka[na*]mpi ivvūr āḻvār vetanāyakar perāl vilaikoṇṭu variniṅkalākki
ivarkku tiruṉāḷ eḻuntaruḷuvittu pontu pattāvatu tirunāḷ••••

ṭṭaṉ viḷaiporuḷḷile ciṟutu peṟṟileṉeṉṟu coṉṉapaṭiyāle ippaṅku
ivaṉ taṉakke kuṭuttom ippaṅkukku āṇṭoṉṟukku uṭaiyār tirune-

lveli uṭaiyāre[k] kaṭamai antarāyam niṅkalāka nūṟṟunālpatiṉ kala
nellukkuṭukka kaṭavatākavum coṉṉom ippaṭikku tāṅkaḷum viṉiyokamum kuṭuttu
ṉellu nūṟṟu nāl(p)patiṉ kalamum koṇṭu tirunāḷ eḻuntaruḷuvikka
vum paṇṇi ippaṭi cantirāticcavaṟ celvatāka kalli[lu]-

[i]vai paḻantiparāyaṉ eḻuttu ivai pallava
rāyaṉ eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 433.
(A.R. No. 433 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, SRIVAIKUNTHAM TALUK, MURAPPANĀḌU.
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH WALL
OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
TRIBH. KOṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ
inscription.p:
This inscription is also mutilated and incomplete. It states that a piece
of land at Murappu-nāḍu alias Sōmidēva-chaturvēdimaṅgalam which had been
assigned as kārāṇmai to Āli Villavadaraiyaṉ of Vaḻugūr with the kaḍamai thereon
being paid to the (Śiva) temple of Yōgīśvaram-uḍaiyār at the place was
transferred by Vēṇāḍuḍaiyāṉ without the consent of the former, to the temple
of Vēdanāyaka-viṇṇagar-āḻvār. As compensation for the same, Vēṇāḍuḍaiyār
now purchased (3/4) vēli and one mā of land at Maṇakkuḍi, the eastern hamlet of
Śrīvallabhamaṅgalam, and made it over as kārāṇmai to Villavadaraiyar and the
kaḍamai to the Āḻvār temple. This is evidently the same land that is mentioned
in No. 431 above.

l.ta:
ṟṟil vaḻukūruṭaiyā ṉāli villavataraiyaṉ kāṇiyāṉa cuntarapāṇṭiyaṉ
kolāl nilam nālumāvum niṅkalākap piṭicūḻntu innilam villavataraiyaṉukku
kārāṇmaiyum ivvūr

yār koyilile kallum veṭṭi ikkalveṭṭiṉpaṭi kaṭamaiyiṟut taṉupavit
tup potacceyte yinnilattai veṇāṭuṭaiyāṉ ivvillavataraiyaṉaik keḷāte
yivaṉ kārāṇmaiyai inta āḻvāṟku nirvārttu innilattukku

ḷakkūṟṟattu śrīvallavamaṅkalattuk kiḻpiṭākai maṇakkuṭip puṟakaraiyil
veṇāṭuṭaiyāṉ vilaikoṇṭu ivvillavataraiyaṉukkuk kārāṇmaiyum inta veta
nāyaka viṇṇakarāḻvārkkuk kaṭamaiyumākac cuntarapāṇṭiyaṉ kolā liṭṭa nilam
•••••

nila mukkāle yorumāvukku tirunelveliyuṭaiyār koyil kalveṭṭiṉ
paṭi kaṭamai payir pāṟtup payirkkoṇṭa nilattukku māttāl kalaṉetūṇippatakku
nellu maraimāvaraik kāṇiyc [ca]mpūvaḻi yiṟuttu nampu ceyvārkaḷum śrīvaiṣṇavar
kaiyāl
(īncomplete)

inscription.head:
No. 434.
(A.R. No. 434 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, SRIVAIKUNTHAM TALUK, MURAPPANĀḌU.
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE NORTH-WEST
AND SOUTH WALLS OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
TRIBH. KŌṈĒRIṈMAIKOṆḌĀṈ—11th YEAR AND 493rd DAY
inscription.p:
This records a royal order issued at the request of Maḻavarāyaṉ and
addressed to the authorities of the temple of Sōmidēva-viṇṇagar-āḻvār newly
constructed on the banks of the Taṇporundam (Tāmraparṇi) river by Araiyaṉ
Pūvaṉ Vēṇāḍuḍaiyār of Kīḻai-Koḍumaḷūr alias Madurōdaiyanallūr, making over
certain lands at the place as tax-free dēvadāna, after acquiring them from their
previous owners to meet the requirements of offerings and worship in the temple.
This seems to have some connection with No. 433 above

inscription.head:
No. 435.
(A.R. No. 435 of 1906)
TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT, SRIVAIKUNTHAM TALUK, MURAPPANĀḌU.
VĒDANĀRĀYAṆA-PERUMĀḶ TEMPLE—ON THE SAME WALL
JAṬĀVARMAN VĪRA-PĀṆḌYADĒVA—14th YEAR
inscription.p:
The king claims to have taken Īḻam, Koṅgu, and Śoḻa-nāḍu and performed
the anointment of heroes at Perumbaṟṟappuliyūr. The details of the date are
given as Kaṟkaṭaka, śu. 1, Sunday, Pushya which would be equivalent to 1266 A.D.,
July 4. This registers a deed given by the Mahāsabhā of Pōsaḷavīra Sōmidēva-
chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Murappunāḍu, to god Śrī Narasimha-
Paramasvāmi enshrined in the maṭha of Ēkadaṇḍa-Paramahaṁsa in the village,
confirming the title of the deity to the ownership of some lands in the village,
with their contours and extent specified. This included two plots one of which
had been sold in the 11th year of the king for the formation of a flower
garden and the other granted in the same year as maḍappuṟam by a certain
Śrīkṛishṇa Bhaṭṭaṉ of Iraivānaraiyūr (Elavānāśūr in the South Arcot District),
and also a third which was granted tax-free by order of the king in his
13th year of reign.

edition.l:
[svasti] śrī [||*] koccaṭaiyapaṉmarāṉa tribhuvaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ īḻamuṅ
koṅkuñ coṇāṭuṅ koṇṭu perumpaṟṟappuliyūril virābhiṣēkam paṇṇiaruḷiya
śrī virapāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu 14- vatu kaṟkaṭaka nāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu prathamai
yu[m] nāya[ṟṟukkiḻamaiyu]m peṟṟa [pū]cattunāḷ murappunā[ṭ]ṭu brahmadēyam
śrī pocalavira sōmidēva caturvēdimaṅkalattu mahāsabh[ai*]yo[m*] ivvūril
ēkadaṇḍaparamahamsar maṭattil samārādhatana[ṅ]koṇṭaruḷkiṟa śrī narasiṅhaparamasvāmi
kaḷukku piṭipāṭu paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu innāyaṉāṟku nāṅkaḷ

muṉpu iddēvaṟku 11- vati[l*] vṛścika nāyaṟṟil nāṅkaḷ viṟṟukkuṭutta

uḷḷa nilattile āṟṟaṅkaraiyaruke

inscription.head:
No. 436.
(A.R. No. 436 of 1906)
COIMBATORE DISTRICT, GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, DAṆĀYAKAṈKŌṬṬAI.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE IN THE FORT—ON THE WEST WALL
OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 1270 (mistake for 1271), Virōdhi, Vaigāśi 1, śu. 8
Sunday, Makhā corresponding to A.D. 1349, April 26, f.d.n. ·29.

It is mutilated and slightly built in. It records an order of the temple
authorities of God Tāntōṉṟīśvaram-uḍaiyār at Tuṟavalūr alias Nīlagiri-sādāraṇaṉ-
kōṭṭai in Oḍuvaṅgā-nāḍu, a division of Koṅgu-Maṇḍalam, conferring the hereditary
post of temple architect on a certain Śōṇai-Āḻvār Sthapathi Aḻagiyanāyanār along
with the privileges and perquisites attached thereto.

l.ta: tam tiṟampāmal ceṉṉimel vaittu aruḷāl āti ca[ṇ]ṭecu[raṉ]

edition.l: [ā]teca[m] [||*] śakābdam āyira[*t]tirunūṟṟu eḻupatiṉme[l] cel[lā]-

to[ṉ*]ṟīcuramuṭaiyār koyilil ācāriyak kāṇi āṭci iva••
paṇikkuḷ•••• ṉṟu maṟṟum ācāriyap peṟuḷḷavaiyum pe[ṟa]-
ka cempilum cilaiyilum veṭṭikkoḷḷak kuṭuttom aruḷi-

inscription.head:
No. 437.
(A.R. No. 437 of 1906)
COIMBATORE DISTRICT, GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, DAṆĀYAKAṈKŌṬṬAI.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE IN THE FORT—ON THE SOUTH WALL
OF THE MAṆḌAPA
VĪRA BALLĀḶADĒVA (III)-ŚAKA 1260, PRAMĀTHI.
inscription.p:
This registers a sarvamānya grant of two villages Paṇikkappaḷḷi and Araśēri
in Oḍuvaṅgā-nāḍu, one made in Śaka 1258 (expired) Īśvara, and the other in
the present year Pramāthi, together with all the taxes due thereon as kaḍamai,
to the temple of Tāntōṉṟīśvaramuḍaiyai-nāyanār at Tuṟavalūr alias Nīlagiri-
Sādhāraṇankōṭṭai, by Mādappaṉ Siṅgaya-Daṇṇāyakar, the mahāpradhāni of the
king. Among the kaḍamai dues are mentioned vēḍikkai (entertainment tax ?),
kuṟṟadaṇḍam (fine) and santati-illā-poruḷ (escheat).

edition.l:
svasti śrī [||*] viravallāḷatevar pṛthivirājyam paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa
śakābdam 1258 ṉmel cellāniṉṟa īśvara varuṣattu mācimātam mutal

piṉpu śakābdam 1260- ṉmel pramādisamvatsaram mutal iṉṉāyaṉārkku
iṉṉāṭṭil kuṭutta aṟaiceriyum āka ūr iraṇṭukkum kīḻpāṟkellai

l.ta:
kuruntan[ā]likku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai [āṟu]koṭṭukku vaṭakkum
melpāṟkellai pavaḻa aṟaikku kiḻakkum [va]ṭapāṟkellai nāpa[n]tirantu

kallunāṭṭiṉa ellaikku teṟkum iṉṉāṉkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa naṉcey
puṉcey aṇaikuḷam vāykkāl melṉokkiṉa maram kiḻṉokkiṉa kiṇaṟu teṉpaṭuvarai
miṉmaṭucuṉai poṉ-

paṭukuṭṭam maṟṟu meṟperpaṭṭaṉavum kuṭukkaiyil ivvūrkaḷ iṟai puravu
ciṟṟāyam viṉiyokam veṭikkai tippāṉi tericaṉa kāṇikkai puṟamutal kuṟṟat
teṇṭam

elavai ukavai cumaicuṅkam cārikai veṭṭimuṭṭāvāḷ coṟumāṭṭu
cantatiyillāpporuḷ maṟṟu meṟperpaṭṭa kaṭamaikaḷum iṉṉāyaṉāṟku

inscription.head:
No. 438.
(A.R. No. 438 of 1906)
COIMBATORE DISTRICT, GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, DAṆĀYAKAṈKŌṬṬAI.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE IN THE FORT—ON THE SAME WALL
inscription.p:
This is dated in Śaka 1269, Sarvajit, Tulā, śu. 5 (A.D. 1347, October 9,
Tuesday). The inscription records the appointment of sixteen dēvaraḍiyār women
for honorary service (mariyādi-kāṇiyāṭchi) in the temple of Tāntōṉṟīśvaram-
Uḍaiyār by the temple authorities.

l.ta:
tevaraṭiyār[k*]ku kāṇiyāṭci kuṭuttapaṭi vañci ariyanācci nāyakanācci
ivaḷ makaḷ vaḷattuvāḻvittāḷ kaṟpakanācci piḷḷāṇṭāḷ pirāṭṭi ivaḷ

makaḷ ciṅki civakāmacuntarinācci ivaḷ makaḷ vāḻavantāḷ ivaḷ taṅkai
ulakukkorutti ivaḷ taṅkai vaḷattuvāḻvittāḷ ulaki [k]āmāttāḷ

ipper patiṉāṟum kāṇiyāṭciyāka niṉṟu
cevittu inta aṭaivile tiruvantikkāppum eṭuttu paṇiyum muṟaiyu-

ñceytu muṉṉaraṅkum immariyātiyile āṇṭumāṟi eṟakkaṭavarkaḷākavum
immariyāti kāṇiyāṭciyākac ca

inscription.head:
No. 439.
(A.R. No. 439 of 1906)
COIMBATORE DISTRICT, GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, DAṆĀYAKAṈKŌṬṬAI.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE IN THE FORT—ON THE SAME WALL
inscription.p:
This is dated simply in the cyclic year Virōdhi which might probably
correspond to Śaka 1271, as the writing is similar to that at No. 438 above.
The inscription records the conferment of the post of uvachohakkāṇi in the
temple of Tāntōṉṟīśvaram-Uḍaiyār at Tuṟavalūr by the authorities of the temple,
on a certain Śellappiḷḷaiyāṉ who had been in the service of the temple of
Vāṉava-Nayanār at Rājendraśōḻaṉ-pēṭṭai in Śūralūr-kūṟṟam a division of Rāja-
gambhīra-vaḷanāḍu on the south bank (of the river Kāvēri ?) in Śōḻamaṇḍalam.

l.ta:
cūḻ vaiyyakattu kaṇṭicaṉ karumam ārāyka
paṇṭe aṟañceytān cey-

tān aṟaṅkāttāṉ pātam tiṟa[m*]pāmal ceṉṉimel vaittu aruḷāl
āticaṇṭe

edition.l: raṉ virōdhisamvatsarattu āṉi mātam 14- n tiyatināḷ coḻamaṇṭa-

inscription.head:
No. 440.
(A.R. No. 440 of 1906)
COIMBATORE DISTRICT, GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, DAṆĀYAKAṈKŌṬṬAI.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE IN THE FORT—ON THE NORTH WALL
OF THE MAṆḌAPA
inscription.p:
This is dated in the cyclic year Sarvajit which would correspond to
Śaka 1269, on which date Mahāpradhāni Immaḍi Rāhuttarāya Siṅgaiya-Daṇṇāyakar
is stated to have granted to the temple of Tāntōṉṟīśvaramuḍaiya-Nāyanār at
Nīlagiri-Sadhāraṇaṉkōṭṭai, the tax taṟiiṟai on the looms due from the weavers
residing in the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam streets round the temple and the duty on cloth
(ilāñjinaippēṟ) sold in shops. He also presented to the temple a coracle or
ferry boat (for plying passengers across the river) on the southern bank.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] śrīmaṉu mahāpradāṉi immaṭi irākuttarāyaṉ ciṅkaiya
teṇṇāyakkar nilakiri-

l.ta:
ttā[r*]kku nam olai kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu innāyaṉār tirumaṭaiviḷākattu
irukkum neyyum kaikkoḷa[r]

uḷḷavarkaḷukku taṟiiṟaiāyamum kaṭaiyil viṟkum puṭavaikku ilāñciṉai
uḷḷa mutalum iṉṉāyaṉā-

māṉiyamāka kuṭut
tom ippaṭikku inta olai cā[ta*]ṉamā

••• ttut teṟkiṟ tuṟaiyile oru paṟacilum iṉṉāyaṉāṟku sarvamānnyamākak

inscription.head:
No. 441.
(A.R. No. 441 of 1906)
COIMBATORE DISTRICT, GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, DAṆĀYAKAṈKŌṬṬAI.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE IN THE FORT—ON THE SAME WALL
inscription.p:
The inscription gives the details of date as Pingaḷa, Tai 25, Sunday,
Chaturdaśi, Tiruvoṇam, ardhōdaya corresponding to A.D. 1498, January 21,
f.d.n. ·22.

This records a gift of 45 poṉ for the expenses of offerings during the
daily [ardha]yāma service in the temple of Tāntōṉṟīśvaram-Uḍaiyār at Tuṟavalūr,
with the interest thereon, by Dēvarasaiyaṉ Maṅgarasaiyaṉ a karaṇika-niyōgi of
of Śevvūr in Āṟu-nāḍu, who deposited the amount with the three Sthānika-
Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple.

edition.l:
kanṛpan āṇṭu 1419- kkumel cellāniṉṟa piṅkaḷa varuṣam tai mātam 25 tiyati
nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaiyum caturddaśiyum

l.ta:
puṇṇiya kālattile
koṭṭaicūḻnta āṟunāṭṭu cevvūril karaṇikka

m paṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu iṉṉāyiṉāṟku
nām kaṟpitta kaṭṭiḷai nāḷ oṉṟukku jāmat[tu*] avacarattukku ṉai

edition.l:

inscription.head:
No. 442.
(A.R. No. 442 of 1906)
COIMBATORE DISTRICT, GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, DAṆĀYAKAṈKŌṬṬAI.
RUINED ŚIVA TEMPLE IN THE FORT—ON THE SAME WALL
inscription.p:
This inscription begins with a call for the protection of the grant by
Mahāpradhāni Immaḍi Rāhuttarāya Śiṅgaya-Daṇṇāyakar, and is stated in the
cyclic year Vyaya which corresponds to Śaka 1268. It introduces the merchant
guild Vaiśyavāṇiya-Nagarattār, also called the Danmadāvaḷar with a grandiose
panegyric of their far-flung activities and other excellences, and records an
agreement given by this body to contribute as paṭṭanappagudi a specified amount
in cash levied annually from among their families and on articles of merchandise
like women's cloths, pepper, areca-nut; yarn, salt, cereals, calves (?) and horse,
towards the expenses of a festival in the month of Tai, in the temple of
Tāntōṉṟīśvaramuḍaiyār at Turuvaḷūr alias Śitakaragaṇḍan-kōṭṭai in Oḍuvaṅga-nāḍu.

edition.l: ciṅkayadaṇṇāyakkar rakṣai [||*] pūmātum jayamātum pukaḻmātum

inscription.head:
No. 443.
(A.R. No. 443 of 1906)
COIMBATORE DISTRICT, GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, DAṆĀYAKAṈKŌṬṬAI.
VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE EAST WALL OF THE MAṆḌAPA
inscription.p:
These are two verses of Sanskrit, one invoking the blessing of Vishṇu
in His Boar-incarnation, and the other in praise of Sun-God on whose appearance
at down the worldlike lotus flower blossoms into life.

l.gr:
svastiśrī hariḥ ādyaḥ krōḍākratiryuṣmānviṣṇuḥ puṣṇātu sarvadā |
dhattē viśvambharā yasya damṣṭrāgrē narttakiśriyam [||*]

nirvighnamastu | tatōkhilajagat patmabōdhāyā cyutabhānunā | dēvaka
pūrvasandhyāyā māvirbhūtammahātmanā [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 444.
(A.R. No. 444 of 1906)
COIMBATORE DISTRICT, GOPICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, DAṆĀYAKAṈKŌṬṬAI
VISHṆU TEMPLE—ON THE SOUTH AND EAST WALLS
OF THE MAṆḌAPA
VĪRA BALLĀḶADĒVA (III)-ŚAKA 1245
inscription.p:
The details of date are given as Rudhirōdgāri, Mīna, śu. 1, Sunday,
Utiraṭṭādi., which would correspond to A.D. 1324, February 26.

Some portions of the inscription are lost. It gives the praśasti of king
Hoysaḷa Vīra Vallāḷadēva who is stated to have been ruling from his capital
Uṇṇāmalaippaṭṭanam, and of his subordinate chief Vīra Siddha Kētaya-Daṇḍ-
nayaka, son of Vīra Mādhava Daṇḍanāyaka, by whom this temple of Śrī Mādhava-
Perumāḷ at Nīlagiri Sādhāraṇankōṭṭai in Uḍuvakkā nāḍu is said to have been
constructed. It registers a grant by the Nāṭṭavar of the several divisions of
Eḻugarai-nāḍu of a village each in their jurisdiction as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam, for meeting
the requirements of worship and offerings to the deity daily and on special
days in the year like Vishu-ayana, Saṅkrama, Amāvāsyā, and Chaitra-pavitram
festival. Of the villages granted the following may be mentioned 1. Muḍiturai
in Vaḍa Pariśāra-nāḍu 2. Kāṅgaiyam in Uḍuvakkā-nāḍu 3. Tirukkaḍambūr in
Vaḍagarai-nāḍu 4. Puliyūr in Pūṅguḍi-nāḍu 5. Īṅgaiyūr in Pūndurai-nāḍu
6. Purav[lūi] in Karuppu-nāḍu 7. Kondaḻam in Arrīyā-nāḍu 8, Uṛṛalūr in
Poṅgalūrkkā-nāḍu 9. Puttūr-nāḍu (?) in Pērūr-nāḍu. (10-11) a village ēach in
Gāṅgēya nāḍu and Viḷai-nāḍu and 12. Vadambaśēri.
